papers.title: Paradigm Shift from Statistical Channel Modeling to Digital Twin Prediction: An Environment-Generalizable ChannelLM for 6G AI-enabled Air Interface
papers.authors: Yichen Cai, Yuelong Qiu, Jianhua Zhang, Li Yu, Yuxiang Zhang, Zhen Zhang, Guangyi Liu
papers.affiliations: State Key Lab of Networking and Switching Technology | Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications | Inner Mongolia Key Laboratory of Intelligent Communication | Future Research Laboratory, China Mobile | Research Institute, Beijing | telecommunication union radiocommunication sector (ITU-R)
papers.abstract: As 6G advances, ubiquitous connectivity and higher capacity requirements of the air interface pose substantial challenges for accurate and real-time wireless channel acquisition in diverse environments. Conventional statistical channel modeling relies on offline measurement data from limited environments, struggling to support online applications facing diverse environments. To this end, the digital twin channel (DTC) has emerged as a novel paradigm that constructs a digital replica of the physical environment through high-fidelity sensing and predicts corresponding channel in real time utilizing artificial intelligence (AI) models. As the engine of DTC, existing AI models struggle to simultaneously achieve strong environmental generalization in real-world and end-to-end channel prediction for real time tasks. Therefore, this paper proposes a channel large model (ChannelLM)-driven DTC architecture comprising three modules: low-complexity and high-accuracy environment reconstruction based on dynamic object detection and multimodal alignment of image and point cloud data, physically interpretable environment feature extraction, and a ChannelLM core to mapping these features into generalized environment representations for multi-task channel prediction. Simulation results demonstrate that, in unseen test environments, compared with small-scale AI models, ChannelLM reduces prediction errors by 4.23 dB in channel state information prediction while achieving an end-to-end inference latency of 70 milliseconds in the real world.
papers.summary_cn: 随着6G发展，泛在连接和更高容量需求对多场景精确实时无线信道获取带来重大挑战。传统统计信道建模依赖有限环境的离线测量数据，难以支撑面临多样化环境的在线应用。数字孪生信道(DTC)因此兴起，通过高保真感知构建物理环境数字副本，并利用AI模型实时预测信道。作为DTC引擎，现有AI模型难以同时实现强环境泛化与端到端实时信道预测。因此，本文提出通道大模型(ChannelLM)驱动的DTC架构，包含三个模块：基于动态目标检测与图像点云多模态对齐的低复杂度高精度环境重建、物理可解释的环境特征提取，以及将特征映射为泛化环境表示用于多任务信道预测的ChannelLM核心。仿真结果表明，在未见测试环境中，相比小规模AI模型，ChannelLM在信道状态信息预测中降低误差4.23dB，同时实现端到端推理延迟70毫秒。

strengths: ChannelLM-driven digital twin architecture is a novel and ambitious proposal bridging LLM concepts with 6G channel modeling
Three-module pipeline (environment reconstruction → physical feature extraction → ChannelLM prediction) is well-motivated and logically sound
Quantitative claims: 4.23 dB CSI prediction improvement and 70ms end-to-end inference latency are practically compelling
Environmental generalization to unseen scenarios addresses a critical gap in current DTC research
limitations: Results based on simulation only — no real-world measurement validation for channel prediction accuracy
70ms inference latency claim for 'real world' needs clarification on hardware requirements and whether environment reconstruction is included
The LLM metaphor may be superficial — unclear if genuine large-scale pretraining is performed or if this is a transformer architecture labeled as 'LM'
Multimodal alignment of image and point cloud data adds complexity that may not be justified for channel prediction alone
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Simulation-based evaluation with unseen test environments. No real-world measurement campaign reported. The claimed 70ms real-world inference latency requires hardware specification to assess feasibility.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Strong novelty in framing and architecture design. The 6G AI-native channel modeling direction is high-impact. Main weakness is simulation-only validation — real measurement data would significantly strengthen the paper. The 'ChannelLM' branding is ambitious and needs technical substance to match.

papers.title: A Novel CSI-RS Reporting Scheme for RIS Optimization in O-RAN-based NextG Networks
papers.authors: Ali Fuat Sahin, Sefa Kayraklik, Ali Gorcin, Ibrahim Hokelek, Ertugrul Basar, Halim Yanikomeroglu
papers.affiliations: Communications and Signal Processing Research (HISAR) | Lab, TUBITAK BILGEM, Kocaeli, Turkiye | Faculty of Electrical and Electronics Engineering | Istanbul Technical University, Istanbul, Turkiye | §Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering | Koç University, Istanbul, Turkiye
papers.abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) technology is a promising enabler for next-generation (NextG) wireless systems, capable of dynamically shaping the propagation environment. Integrating RIS within the open radio access network (O-RAN) architecture enables flexible and intelligent control of wireless links. However, practical RIS-assisted operation requires efficient acquisition and reporting of channel state information (CSI) to support real-time control from the base station side. This paper proposes a CSI reference signal (CSI-RS)-based reporting scheme for downlink complex channel information (CCI) to facilitate RIS optimization in an O-RAN-compliant environment. The proposed framework establishing CCI extraction and CSI-RS reporting procedures is experimentally validated on a real-world testbed integrating an open-source O-RAN system with an RIS prototype operating in the n78 frequency band. Existing channel estimation-based RIS optimization algorithms, including Hadamard and orthogonal matching pursuit (OMP), are tailored for integration into the O-RAN architecture. Experimental results demonstrate notable improvements in received signal power for both near and far users, highlighting the effectiveness and practical viability of the proposed scheme.
papers.summary_cn: 可重构智能表面(RIS)技术是下一代无线系统有前景的使能技术，能动态塑造传播环境。将RIS集成到开放无线接入网络(O-RAN)架构可实现无线链路的灵活智能控制。然而，实际RIS辅助操作需要高效获取和上报信道状态信息(CSI)以支持基站实时控制。本文提出基于CSI参考信号(CSI-RS)的下行复信道信息(CCI)上报方案，以在O-RAN兼容环境中促进RIS优化。该框架建立CCI提取和CSI-RS上报流程，在集成开源O-RAN系统与工作于n78频段的RIS原型机的真实测试平台上实验验证。现有的基于信道估计的RIS优化算法，包括Hadamard和正交匹配追踪(OMP)，被适配集成到O-RAN架构。实验结果表明近远用户接收信号功率均有显著提升，凸显了所提方案的有效性和实际可行性。

strengths: Real-world testbed integration of open-source O-RAN with RIS prototype in n78 band — strong practical validation
CSI-RS-based CCI reporting scheme designed specifically for O-RAN compliance is practically valuable
Adaptation of existing channel estimation algorithms (Hadamard, OMP) to O-RAN architecture shows engineering maturity
Demonstrated improvements for both near and far users addresses practical coverage concerns
limitations: CSI-RS reporting for RIS is a well-studied problem; the O-RAN integration angle adds incremental novelty
Hadamard and OMP are classical baselines — comparison with learning-based RIS optimization methods would strengthen the evaluation
Abstract does not quantify the improvement magnitude (e.g., dB gain, throughput increase)
Limited to downlink only; uplink CSI-RS reporting for RIS is equally important but not addressed
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Strong — real-world testbed with actual O-RAN stack and RIS prototype at n78. This is the paper's biggest strength. However, the abstract lacks specific quantitative results.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Practical engineering contribution with real testbed validation, which is increasingly valued. Novelty is moderate as the core techniques are adaptations of known methods. The O-RAN integration angle is timely but not sufficient alone for high novelty. Good fit for an industry-focused venue.

papers.title: Geometry-Aware Networking for Low-Altitude Economy: Movable Antennas in Space-Air-Ground Integrated Systems
papers.authors: Heyou Liu, Bang Huang, Mohamed-Slim Alouini
papers.affiliations: are emerging as a key foundation for future non-terrestrial | Division, King Abdullah University of Science and Technology | challenges and future research directions. Our goal is
papers.abstract: Space--air--ground integrated networks (SAGINs) are emerging as a key foundation for future non-terrestrial networks (NTNs) and low-altitude economy services. However, their performance is increasingly limited not only by communication resources, but by the inability to adapt to rapidly changing spatial geometry. Here, spatial geometry refers to the relative configuration among network nodes, obstacles, and targets, which directly determines propagation conditions, blockage states, interference patterns, and sensing observability.This trend becomes more pronounced as low-altitude operations grow in density and complexity, causing the dominant bottleneck to shift from static resource allocation toward real-time maintenance of favorable spatial geometry across layers.In this article, we argue that movable antenna (MA) technology provides a fundamentally new perspective for SAGIN design. By enabling controlled antenna displacement, MA introduces a spatial degree of freedom that allows the network to directly adapt local spatial geometry at fine granularity, rather than passively reacting to it through beamforming or platform mobility.We present a geometry-aware, layered SAGIN architecture, where Low-Earth-Orbit (LEO) provides macro-scale coverage and coordination, High-Altitude Platform Stations (HAPS) enables regional continuity and backhaul support, and MA is incorporated into the layered design to enable fine-grained geometry adaptation, particularly at unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and terrestrial layers where local channel dynamics are most pronounced. We further discuss how such geometry control enhances robustness, supports multi-functional operation spanning communication, sensing, control, and navigation, and enables more flexible spatial cooperation across layers.
papers.summary_cn: 空-天-地一体化网络(SAGIN)正成为未来非地面网络(NTN)和低空经济服务的基础。然而，其性能日益受限于通信资源以及无法适应快速变化的空间几何。这里的空间几何指网络节点、障碍物和目标之间的相对配置，直接决定传播条件、遮蔽状态、干扰模式和感知可观测性。随着低空作业密度和复杂度增长，这一趋势愈发明显，使主导瓶颈从静态资源分配转向跨层实时维护有利空间几何。本文认为，可移动天线(MA)技术为SAGIN设计提供了全新视角。通过实现受控天线位移，MA引入空间自由度，使网络能够在细粒度上直接适应局部空间几何，而非通过波束成形或平台移动被动反应。我们提出几何感知的分层SAGIN架构，其中低地球轨道(LEO)提供宏覆盖和协调，高空平台站(HAPS)实现区域连续性和回传支持，MA被纳入分层设计以实现细粒度几何适配，特别是在无人机(UAV)和地面层，那里局部信道动态最为显著。我们进一步讨论这种几何控制如何增强鲁棒性，支持跨越通信、感知、控制和导航的多功能操作，并实现跨层更灵活的空间协作。

strengths: Geometry-aware networking perspective for SAGIN is a thoughtful reframing of the design problem
Movable antenna technology as a spatial degree of freedom is a genuinely new design dimension
Layered architecture (LEO → HAPS → UAV → terrestrial) with MA integration at local layers is conceptually clean
Multi-functional operation spanning communication, sensing, control, and navigation is forward-looking
limitations: This is a perspective/survey article — no analytical framework, simulation results, or experimental validation
No quantitative analysis of geometry adaptation gains over conventional beamforming or platform mobility
MA technology for UAVs faces severe SWaP (size, weight, and power) constraints that are not addressed
The argument that geometry is 'the dominant bottleneck' is asserted but not supported with evidence or analysis
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: No data — this is a conceptual/architectural paper. No simulations, no measurements, no numerical comparisons.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: A well-articulated vision paper that may stimulate future research but lacks the technical depth expected of a primary research contribution. Without any quantitative validation or analytical rigor, it is closer to an invited article or magazine paper than a research paper.

papers.title: A Novel Piecewise Atmospheric Attenuation Model for Free Space Optical Links in Vertical Heterogeneous Networks
papers.authors: Eylem Erdogan, Mohammed Elamassie, Ibrahim Altunbas, Gunes Karabulut Kurt, Murat Uysal, Halim Yanikomeroglu
papers.affiliations: Izmir Institute of Technology, Turkiye. M | Ozyegin University, Turkiye. I. Altunbas is | with the Istanbul Technical University, Turkiye. G. K. Kurtiswith | the Polytechnique Montréal, Canada. M. Uysalisthewith the New
papers.abstract: Free-space optical (FSO) communication is emerging as a key backhaul technology for next-generation vertical heterogeneous networks (VHetNets), whose architecture spans satellites, high-altitude platform stations (HAPS), unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), and terrestrial nodes. Along these vertical and slant paths, optical beams traverse successive atmospheric layers that may contain clouds, fog, rain, and aerosols, conditions that conventional single-coefficient Beer-Lambert models typically handle only in isolation. Instead of such simplified formulas, we present a unified attenuation model that incorporates aerosols, fog, rain, cloud layers, and drizzle, accounts for the zenith angle, and provides a holistic estimate of the cumulative power loss across atmospheric layers. Numerical results show several-decibel attenuation variations across representative weather scenarios, while the difference between the proposed model predictions and the layer-resolved MODTRAN simulations remains within 1 dB, thereby validating the accuracy of the proposed model and its practical relevance for VHetNet link-budget studies.
papers.summary_cn: 自由空间光通信正成为下一代垂直异构网络的关键回传技术，其架构涵盖卫星、高空平台站、无人机和地面节点。光束在垂直和倾斜路径上穿过可能含云、雾、雨和气溶胶的连续大气层，传统单系数比尔-朗伯模型通常只能孤立处理。我们提出一种统一衰减模型，整合了气溶胶、雾、雨、云层和毛毛雨，考虑天顶角，全面估计跨大气层的累积功率损耗。数值结果表明典型天气下衰减变化达数分贝，且所提模型预测与分层MODTRAN模拟差异保持在1 dB内，验证了其准确性及在链路预算研究中的实用价值。

strengths: Unified piecewise model covering aerosols, fog, rain, clouds, drizzle is a genuinely comprehensive approach
Validation against MODTRAN (industry-standard atmospheric simulation) with <1 dB difference is a strong credibility signal
Accounts for zenith angle — this is essential for slant-path VHetNet links
Applicable to full VHetNet vertical stack: satellite, HAPS, UAV, terrestrial — broad scope
Results show several-dB variations across weather scenarios with practical implications for link-budget studies
limitations: Model formulation appears to be an engineering extension rather than fundamental theoretical contribution; mathematical novelty is modest
No experimental validation with real FSO measurement data — MODTRAN is a simulator, not real-world measurement
Performance in extreme edge cases (e.g., heavy fog + rain + clouds simultaneously) not addressed
VHetNet application focus is implied but the model doesn't explicitly account for platform mobility dynamics
Layer boundary definitions (where one atmospheric region ends and another begins) may be a sensitivity parameter not thoroughly discussed
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Relies on MODTRAN simulations for validation. No real-world FSO measurement data. While MODTRAN is a respected industry-standard tool, cross-validation against empirical measurement data would strengthen the contribution significantly.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Solid engineering model with <1 dB MODTRAN validation. Unified multi-layer model fills a real gap in VHetNet link-budget tools. Score tempered by: lack of real-world data validation, modest theoretical novelty (engineered extension of Beer-Lambert), and primary contribution being link-budget analysis rather than a new communications technique.

papers.title: FARM: Foundational Aerial Radio Map for Intelligent Low-Altitude Networking
papers.authors: Shijian Gao, Jiahui Liang, Yifeng Yuan, Wenlihan Lu, Guobin Shen, Liuqing Yang
papers.affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou) | GuangzhouP.R. China | Recent advances in foundation models have redefined AI [19, 20], opening new | datasets, foundation models [21, 22] learn transferable representations that | have begun to demonstrate the potential of foundation models, while no existing
papers.abstract: Precise aerial radio environment characterization is vital for low-altitude planning. However, existing datasets and estimation methods lack the high-resolution granularity required for complex aerial spaces. Additionally, current schemes suffer from poor generalization and heavy reliance on environmental priors. To address these gaps, this paper introduces FARM, a pioneering foundation model for unified aerial radio map estimation. This model is supported by a newly curated, high-resolution dataset featuring multi-band and multi-antenna configurations specifically for low-altitude environments. FARM utilizes a masked autoencoder to extract deep latent representations of the aerial radio environment, which then guide a diffusion-based decoder to generate high-fidelity signal distributions through iterative refinement. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FARM significantly outperforms state-of-the-art benchmarks and exhibits superior generalization capabilities across unseen scenarios. Ultimately, FARM serves as a critical infrastructure for low-altitude economy by enabling autonomous aerial logistics and intelligent urban networking.
papers.summary_cn: 精确的空中无线电环境表征对低空规划至关重要。然而，现有数据集和估计方法缺乏复杂空中空间所需的高分辨率粒度，且当前方案泛化能力差、严重依赖环境先验。为解决这些不足，本文提出FARM，一个用于统一空中无线电地图估计的先驱基础模型。该模型由新构建的高分辨率数据集支撑，包含专门针对低空环境的多频段和多天线配置。FARM利用掩码自编码器提取空中无线电环境深度潜在表示，引导基于扩散的解码器通过迭代细化生成高保真信号分布。大量实验表明，FARM显著优于最先进的基准，并在未见场景中表现出优异泛化能力。最终，FARM作为低空经济关键基础设施，支持自主空中物流和智能城市组网。

strengths: Foundation model paradigm applied to aerial radio map estimation is a timely direction
Masked autoencoder + diffusion decoder architecture is a principled choice for learning spatial signal distributions
Multi-band and multi-antenna high-resolution dataset for low-altitude environments fills a real gap
Claims superior generalization across unseen scenarios, which is a key practical requirement
limitations: MAE + diffusion is a well-established pipeline adapted to this domain rather than fundamentally new
Abstract lacks quantitative specifics on dataset scale (number of environments, spatial resolution, bands used)
Computational cost of diffusion-based inference may limit real-time deployment
Generalization claims need scrutiny on what 'unseen' means — same city different area, or entirely new urban morphology?
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Claims a newly curated high-resolution dataset with multi-band and multi-antenna configurations, which is valuable. However, abstract does not specify whether data is simulation-based, ray-traced, or from real measurements — a critical distinction for practical impact.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Addresses a timely problem (low-altitude economy radio mapping) with a modern ML architecture. Foundation model framing is appealing but the technical novelty is moderate. Dataset contribution could be the paper's strongest asset if real measurements are included.

papers.title: Two-Tier High Altitude Platform Stations (HAPS) for Exploring Wireless Energy Harvesting
papers.authors: Faicel Khennoufa, Khelil Abdellatif, Halim Yanikomeroglu, Safwan Alfattani, Metin Ozturk, Ferdi Kara
papers.affiliations: research on 6G communication networks is shifting its | Avancées (ENSTA), Department of Basic Training in Computer | LGEERE Laboratory, Department of Electrical | Engineering, Echahid Hamma Lakhdar University, El-Oued, Al | with Non-Terrestrial Network (NTN) Laboratory, Department of | Systems and Computer Engineering, Carleton University, Ottawa
papers.abstract: In sixth-generation (6G) cellular networks and beyond, aerial platforms, such as uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) and high-altitude platform stations (HAPS), are anticipated to play a crucial role in enhancing connectivity, expanding network coverage, and supporting advanced communication services. However, the deployment of energy-efficient onboard communication systems is essential for their widespread adoption and effectiveness. The integration of energy harvesting (EH) into aerial platforms is envisioned to be pivotal in promoting both energy and cost efficiency. In this paper, we propose a new paradigm for aerial platforms in which they can collect energy from the transmitted signals of nearby aerial platforms. The paper employs a two-tier architecture with HAPS super-macro base stations (HAPS-SMBS) system: regular HAPS-SMBS nodes serve as base stations, while a "mother" HAPS-SMBS node acts as a manager to coordinate communications between regular HAPS-SMBS and the ground station, thus enabling wireless energy transfer. Specifically, we analyze the characteristics of EH-enabled HAPS-SMBS and compare their performance with those without EH. Additionally, we derive the optimal regular HAPS-SMBS positioning to mitigate signal attenuation and power loss. Subsequently, we formulate a joint optimization problem for regular HAPS-SMBS positioning and the EH factor. We solve the problem using the iterative distance and EH factor algorithm (IDFA); however, we employ $Q$-learning to verify its effectiveness. Our findings indicate that, compared to conventional EH systems, IDFA and $Q$-learning exhibit higher data rate performance. In contrast, $Q$-learning outperforms IDFA systems in linear modelswith intensive training in approximating optimal values. Furthermore, maximizing transmit power achieves higher gains than systems without EH.
papers.summary_cn: 在6G及未来网络中，无人机和高空平台站对增强连接至关重要。集成能量收集（EH）是提升其能效的关键。本文提出新范式，使空中平台能从邻近平台信号中收集能量。采用两层HAPS超级宏基站架构：常规节点作基站，“母”节点协调其与地面站通信以实现无线传能。我们分析了支持EH的HAPS-SMBS特性，推导了最优常规节点位置以减轻衰减，并构建了位置与EH因子的联合优化问题。使用迭代距离与EH因子算法（IDFA）求解，并用Q学习验证。结果表明，IDFA和Q学习比传统EH系统数据速率更高，Q学习在近似最优值上优于IDFA。最大化发射功率比无EH系统增益更高。

strengths: Novel two-tier HAPS architecture with 'mother' node coordination is architecturally interesting
Cross-HAPS wireless energy harvesting concept is a fresh take on aerial platform sustainability
IDFA iterative algorithm combined with Q-learning verification provides dual-method validation
Joint optimization of HAPS positioning + EH factor is a practical and well-motivated problem
Addresses genuine 6G concern: energy efficiency of aerial platforms for prolonged deployment
Compares IDFA vs. Q-learning with discussion of training intensity tradeoffs
limitations: No real-world dataset used — purely simulation-based analysis with assumed parameters
Q-learning approach lacks sufficient details: state space, action space, reward function not clearly specified
Performance comparison (IDFA vs. Q-learning) appears based on training convergence observation, not rigorous benchmarking
EH from transmitted signals of other aerial platforms: power budget and energy gain tradeoffs not clearly quantified
Two-tier architecture assumptions (single 'mother' node, specific coordination model) may not scale to practical multi-HAPS deployments
No discussion of the energy cost of the coordination/management overhead from the mother HAPS node
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: No real-world datasets mentioned. The Q-learning verification uses simulated scenarios without external data validation. The comparison between IDFA and Q-learning lacks standardized benchmarks and quantitative performance metrics.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Conceptually novel two-tier HAPS architecture with cross-platform energy harvesting. Strong motivation for 6G aerial connectivity. However, moderate rigor (Q-learning details sparse, no real data) and below-average data quality (no real datasets) drag the score down despite good novelty and high practical impact potential.

papers.title: Enabling Safety-Critical Wireless Communications via Safe Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Haoran Peng, Tong Wu, Hang Liu, Weijia Zheng, Ying-Jun Angela Zhang, Anna Scaglione
papers.affiliations: the Department of Information Engineering, The Chinese University of as | Department of Electrical spatially | and Computer Engineering, University of Central Florida, Orlando, FLUSA intractable | of Internet of Things for Smart City and the Department of Electrical | and Computer Engineering, University of Macau, Macao S.A.R. (mixed | Department of Electrical
papers.abstract: Ensuring strict safety guarantees is the paramount challenge for emerging 5G/6G wireless systems, particularly as they increasingly govern mission-critical applications ranging from autonomous UAV swarms to industrial automation. While deep reinforcement learning (DRL) offers a promising solution for complex resource allocation, standard algorithms frequently violate essential constraints, such as QoS mandates and power limits, posing unacceptable risks of system failure and regulatory non-compliance. We propose Safe-Deep Q-Learning, a novel algorithm that simultaneously addresses all three challenges: it handles mixed-integer nonconvex problems by approximating the Q-function, adapts to stochastic dynamics, and enforces dual-timescale constraints using integrated Lagrangian methods. Our framework features adaptive penalty scaling and constraint violation tracking, specifically tailored for wireless environments, and is designed to operate in both distributed and centralized architectural modes. We prove convergence to optimal constraint-satisfying policies under mild conditions and demonstrate robustness through dual variable stabilization. Validation on unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarm control network and post-disaster emergency communications applications shows that Safe-Deep Q-Learning achieves stringent adherence to safety bounds with near-zero violation rates, significantly outperforming existing constrained RL baselines, establishing its effectiveness for safety-critical wireless deployments.
papers.summary_cn: 确保严格的安全保障是5G/6G无线系统的首要挑战，因其日益主导无人机群控等关键任务。深度强化学习虽能解决复杂资源分配，但常违反QoS和功率等约束，带来系统故障风险。我们提出Safe-Deep Q-Learning算法，通过近似Q函数处理混合整数非凸问题，适应随机动态，并利用集成拉格朗日方法执行双时间尺度约束。该框架具备自适应惩罚缩放和违规跟踪功能，专为无线环境定制，支持分布式和集中式模式。我们在温和条件下证明了其收敛性。在无人机群控和灾后应急通信的验证表明，该算法以近零违规率严格遵守安全边界，显著优于现有约束RL基线，确立了其在安全关键无线部署中的有效性。

strengths: Safe-Deep Q-Learning is a well-motivated, technically novel algorithm addressing a critical safety gap in DRL for wireless
Dual-timescale constraint enforcement via integrated Lagrangian methods is technically sound
Adaptive penalty scaling and constraint violation tracking specifically tailored for wireless dynamics is a strong design choice
Convergence proofs under mild conditions — theoretical grounding is solid and uncommon in safety-critical RL papers
Dual variable stabilization analysis addresses a real training instability issue in constrained RL
Evaluated on UAV swarm control AND post-disaster emergency communications — two highly relevant real-world scenarios
Distributed and centralized architectural modes covered, supporting practical deployment flexibility
Near-zero violation rates reported — this is the key claim for safety-critical applications
limitations: Simulations on UAV swarm and emergency communications are synthetic/constructed scenarios; no real-world sensor data or hardware-in-the-loop validation
Near-zero violation rates may be scenario-dependent; robustness across diverse wireless channel models not systematically tested
Adaptive penalty scaling hyperparameter sensitivity not fully explored
Distributed mode implementation assumes synchronized communication which may not hold in practice for UAV swarms
Algorithm complexity and computational overhead for edge deployment not discussed
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: UAV swarm and emergency communications validation scenarios are meaningful but appear synthetic. No external dataset reference. The key quantitative claims (near-zero violation rates, convergence to optimal policies) are from self-generated simulations — reproducibility depends on implementation details not fully disclosed.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Top paper in this batch. Addresses a critical real-world problem (safety-critical 5G/6G wireless) with a novel algorithm (Safe-Deep Q-Learning) that has solid theoretical backing (convergence proofs) and dual real-world scenario validation. High impact and strong rigor. Minor deductions for synthetic-only validation and lack of real-world hardware deployment proof.

papers.title: Adaptive Power Allocation and User Scheduling for LEO Satellites using Channel Predictions
papers.authors: Lachlan Drake, Lawrence Ong, Duy T. Ngo
papers.affiliations: L. Drake, L. Ong,and D. T. Ngoarewith the School of Engineering, The | University of Newcastle, Callaghan NSW2308Australia | and the Commonwealth Scientific&Industrial Research Organisation
papers.abstract: Low earth orbit (LEO) satellites are a key technology to enable connectivity for rural and remote users. Communication satellites in LEO can provide coverage to much larger areas than terrestrial or aerial systems, while offering improved data rates when compared with geostationary systems. However, a major challenge with LEO satellite communications is the high mobility of the satellite, which results in a rapidly changing communication channel. Due to this, it is challenging to fairly allocate communication resources to multiple users in the system. This work proposes an Adaptive Power Allocation and Scheduling Scheme (APASS) to ensure user fairness in the downlink of a LEO satellite system serving mobile ground users. First, a novel channel and transmission model is introduced to capture the variability in channel statistics due to the satellite's trajectory. Then, a non-convex optimization problem is formulated to maximize the minimum rate across all ground users over a fixed set of time slots. To solve this problem, the proposed APASS dynamically allocates power and schedules transmissions based on predicted future channel gains. Numerical results show that APASS achieves strong performance even with substantial prediction errors, faring close to an upper bound that assumes perfect future channel knowledge. Furthermore, it improves the minimum user rate by a factor of 2.98 compared to equal-power allocation and maintains user fairness with a Jain's fairness index of well above 0.99.
papers.summary_cn: 低轨（LEO）卫星是实现偏远地区连接的关键技术。相比地面或空中系统，LEO卫星覆盖范围更广；相比地球静止轨道系统，其数据速率更高。然而，LEO卫星的高移动性导致信道快速变化，使得多用户间的公平资源分配极具挑战。本文提出自适应功率分配与调度方案（APASS），以确保服务移动地面用户的LEO卫星下行链路的用户公平性。首先，引入新的信道与传输模型，刻画卫星轨迹引起的信道统计特性变化。然后，构建非凸优化问题，以最大化固定时隙集上所有地面用户的最小速率。APASS基于预测的未来信道增益动态分配功率和调度传输。数值结果表明，即使存在较大预测误差，APASS仍表现优异，接近假设完美未来信道知识的上限。此外，与等功率分配相比，其最小用户速率提升2.98倍，且Jain公平指数远超0.99，保持了用户公平性。

strengths: Well-structured problem formulation with clear mathematical framework
Channel prediction integration is timely for LEO systems
Strong fairness performance with Jain's index >0.99
Handles prediction errors robustly
limitations: Evaluation appears simulation-only with no real LEO channel data
Limited comparison to only equal-power baseline; missing modern schedulers like proportional fair or max-weight
Prediction mechanism not detailed—assumes perfect prediction structure without discussing computational overhead
No hardware or implementation considerations for satellite payload constraints
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Synthetic/simulation-based evaluation only; no real LEO satellite channel measurements or 3GPP NTN channel models mentioned
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Solid theoretical contribution but limited real-world validation and narrow baseline comparison reduce practical confidence

papers.title: Aerial Multi-Functional RIS in Fluid Antennas-Aided Full-Duplex Networks: A Self-Optimized Hybrid Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach
papers.affiliations: meta-level hyperparameter optimization are incorporated into | Li-Hsiang Shen and Yu-Quan Zhengarewith the Department ofCommu | nication Engineering, National Central University, TaoyuanTaiwan
papers.abstract: To address high data traffic demands of sixth-generation (6G) networks, this paper proposes a novel architecture that integrates autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs) and multi-functional reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (MF-RISs) as AM-RIS in fluid antenna (FA)-assisted full-duplex (FD) networks. The AM-RIS provides hybrid functionalities, including signal reflection, amplification, and energy harvesting (EH), potentially improving both signal coverage and sustainability. Meanwhile, FA facilitates fine-grained spatial adaptability at FD-enabled base station (BS), which complements residual self-interference (SI) suppression. We aim at maximizing the overall energy efficiency (EE) by jointly optimizing transmit DL beamforming at BS, UL user power, configuration of AM-RIS, and positions of the FA and AM-RIS. Owing to the hybrid continuous-discrete parameters and high dimensionality of the intractable problem, we have conceived a self-optimized multi-agent hybrid deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework (SOHRL), which integrates multi-agent deep Q-networks (DQN) and multi-agent proximal policy optimization (PPO), respectively handling discrete and continuous actions. To enhance self-adaptability, an attention-driven state representation and meta-level hyperparameter optimization are incorporated, enabling multi-agents to autonomously adjust learning hyperparameters. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed AM-RIS-enabled FA-aided FD networks empowered by SOHRL algorithm. The results reveal that SOHRL outperforms benchmarks of the case without attention mechanism and conventional hybrid/multi-agent/standalone DRL. Moreover, AM-RIS in FD achieves the highest EE compared to half-duplex, conventional rigid antenna arrays, partial EH, and conventional RIS without amplification, highlighting its potential as a compelling solution for EE-aware wireless networks.
papers.summary_cn: 为应对6G高数据流量需求，本文提出融合自主飞行器与多功能可重构智能表面构成AM-RIS的新架构，应用于流体天线辅助全双工网络。AM-RIS具备反射、放大和能量收集混合功能，可同时提升覆盖和可持续性。FA实现FD基站细粒度空间适配，弥补残余自干扰抑制。通过联合优化下行波束成形、上行功率、AM-RIS配置及FA与AM-RIS位置最大化能效。针对混合连续-离散高维难解问题，设计自优化多智能体混合深度强化学习框架(SOHRL)，集成多智能体DQN和PPO分别处理离散和连续动作。引入注意力驱动状态表示和元层级超参数优化增强自适应性。仿真验证AM-RIS使能FA辅助FD网络与SOHRL算法的有效性。结果表明SOHRL优于无注意力机制及传统混合/多智能体/独立DRL基准。FD中AM-RIS能效优于半双工、传统刚性天线阵列、部分能量收集及无放大RIS，凸显其作为能效感知网络的潜力。

papers.title: A Case Study on Energy-Efficient Edge AI Crack Segmentation
papers.authors: Matthias Tschope, Mohamed Moursi, Vladimir Rybalkin, Bo Zhou, Norbert Wehn, Paul Lukowicz
papers.affiliations: Frames/Jwhilemaintainingamean Intersectionover Union(Io U) | the European Union’s Horizon Europe research and innovation programme
papers.abstract: Crack segmentation on edge devices can support continuous infrastructure monitoring and maintenance and thereby help to preserve public safety. Furthermore, autonomous infrastructure monitoring by using Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) can reduce inspection risks, as human operators no longer need to enter hazardous areas. Edge processing reduces the cost of inspection by eliminating the need for high resolution image storage for offline processing and mitigates the security risks and bandwidth requirements of streaming to cloud servers. Edge inference is difficult due to the limited memory and computational capabilities of edge devices, which can affect both accuracy and latency. Furthermore, battery-powered devices are subject to strict power and energy constraints. Together, these limitations impose restrictions on the model size and computational complexity that can be deployed close to the sensor. In recent years, Transformers have achieved state-of-the-art accuracy in a variety of applications, including semantic segmentation. However, Transformer-based models are typically large and computationally intensive, making efficient edge deployment difficult. To address this, we first apply knowledge distillation to enhance the performance of the base models. We then use PTQ to compress the models further. Additionally, we consider the deployment of these models across multiple edge platforms. To maximize energy efficiency, we design and implement a custom hardware architecture for the models on an FPGA. Our results show that Knowledge Distillation (KD) improves all tested U-Net variants. Among the evaluated platforms, the selected FPGA implementation achieves 398 FPS at 204.99 Frames/J while maintaining a mean IoU of 69.42%. In addition, our best model reaches 71.92% mean IoU, which is 8.82 percentage points (pps) higher than the previously reported result on the CrackVision12K dataset.
papers.summary_cn: 边缘设备上的裂缝分割可支持基础设施持续监测与维护，助力公共安全保护。无人机自主基础设施监测可降低巡检风险，减少人员进入危险区域。边缘处理无需高分辨率图像存储用于离线计算，降低了云服务器传输的安全风险与带宽需求。但边缘设备内存与算力有限，边缘推理面临精度与延迟挑战。电池供电设备受严格功率与能量约束，限制可部署模型的规模与计算复杂度。近年Transformer在语义分割等任务达到最优精度，但通常模型庞大、计算密集，难以高效边缘部署。针对此，我们首先应用知识蒸馏提升基模型性能，再利用PTQ进一步压缩模型，并考虑跨多边缘平台部署。为最大化能源效率，我们为模型设计并实现FPGA自定义硬件架构。结果表明知识蒸馏提升了所有测试的U-Net变体。在评估平台中，选定FPGA实现达到398 FPS、204.99 Frames/J能耗，同时保持69.42%平均IoU。此外最佳模型达到71.92%平均IoU，较CrackVision12K数据集先前报告结果提升8.82个百分点。

strengths: Comprehensive edge deployment pipeline: KD + PTQ + FPGA optimization chain
FPGA result (398 FPS, 204.99 FPS/W) is a strong practical demonstration
8.82 pps mean IoU improvement on CrackVision12K dataset over prior SOTA
Multiple U-Net variants tested with ablation, showing systematic evaluation
End-to-end solution from model compression to hardware implementation
limitations: KD and PTQ applied are standard techniques without novel modifications; innovation lies in the integration
FPGA platform selection not fully justified vs. edge GPU/NPU alternatives
CrackVision12K is a single dataset; generalization to other crack/structural defect domains unclear
FPGA synthesis details and resource utilization not discussed in abstract — hard to assess hardware efficiency tradeoffs
No ablation on which KD variant contributed most to accuracy gains
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Uses CrackVision12K dataset with quantitative comparisons to prior reported result; however, no ablation on model type, no cross-dataset validation, and no real-world field deployment results disclosed.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Strong engineering contribution with concrete hardware numbers (398 FPS) and meaningful accuracy improvement (+8.82 pps). Novelty is moderate as KD+PTQ+FPGA pipeline is known, but end-to-end demonstration across multiple U-Net variants and practical FPGA implementation adds value. Well-presented with clear quantitative claims.

overflow_list.title: Look One Step Ahead: Forward-Looking Incentive Design with Strategic Privacy for Proactive Service Provisioning over Air-Ground Integrated Edge Networks
overflow_list.authors: Sicheng Wu, Minghui Liwang, Yangyang Gao, Deqing Wang, Wenbo Zhu, Yiguang Hong, Wei Ni, Seyyedali Hosseinalipour
overflow_list.affiliations: School of Information Science and whichincursunnecessarylatencyandunderutilizestempo | Engineering, Lanzhou University | Gansu, China. M. Liwang (minghuili ralinformationforservicecoordination[10],[11] | Department of Control Science and Engineering,the National Key Laboratory | Science Center for Intelligent | tion, Tongji University, Shanghai, China. D. Wang()is
overflow_list.abstract: In air-ground integrated networks (AGINs), unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) provide on-demand edge services to ground vehicles. Realizing this vision requires carefully designed incentives to coordinate interactions among self-interested participants. This is exacerbated by the dynamic nature of AGINs, where spatio-temporal variations introduce significant uncertainty in matching UAVs and vehicles. Existing real-time service provisioning typically relies on precise trajectory information, raising privacy concerns and incurring decision latency. To address these challenges, we propose look one-step ahead (LOSA), a novel framework for efficient and privacy-aware service provisioning. By exploiting predictable vehicle travel times between intersections, LOSA decomposes the process into two coupled phases: (i) a privacy-aware look-ahead phase and (ii) a lightweight real-time execution phase. The look-ahead phase allows vehicles to adaptively adjust privacy budgets based on historical utility, balancing trajectory exposure and matching accuracy. Leveraging this, a double auction mechanism establishes binding one-step-ahead agreements (OSAAs) through trajectory similarity clustering, while constructing preference lists to hedge against mobility uncertainty. The execution phase then enforces pre-established OSAAs and preference lists, resolving real-time resource conflicts without costly re-negotiations. This design reduces computational overhead and preserves robustness. We analytically corroborate that LOSA guarantees truthfulness, individual rationality, and budget balance. Experiments on real-world datasets (DAIR-V2X, HighD, and RCooper) demonstrate that LOSA achieves superior privacy protection while lowering transaction latency compared to baseline approaches.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 空地一体化网络(AGIN)中，无人机为地面车辆提供按需边缘服务。实现此愿景需精心设计激励机制协调自利参与者交互。AGIN动态特性加剧了这一挑战，时空变化使无人机与车辆匹配面临显著不确定性。现有实时服务供给通常依赖精确轨迹信息，引发隐私担忧并产生决策延迟。针对这些挑战，我们提出前视一步(LOSA)框架，实现高效隐私感知服务供给。LOSA利用交叉口间车辆可预测行驶时间，将过程分解为两个耦合阶段：隐私感知前瞻阶段与轻量实时执行阶段。前瞻阶段允许车辆基于历史效用自适应调整隐私预算，平衡轨迹暴露与匹配精度。借此，双拍卖机制通过轨迹相似性聚类建立约束性前视一步协议(OSAA)，并构建偏好列表以对冲移动不确定性。执行阶段则强制执行预建OSAA与偏好列表，解决实时资源冲突，避免代价高昂的重新协商。该设计降低计算开销并保持鲁棒性。我们从理论上验证LOSA保证真实性、个体理性及预算平衡。真实数据集(DAIR-V2X、HighD、RCooper)实验表明，LOSA在提供更强隐私保护的同时，相比基线方法降低交易延迟。

strengths: Novel dual-phase decomposition (look-ahead + real-time execution) with clear functional separation
Privacy budget adaptation based on historical utility is an elegant design choice
Double auction mechanism with OSAAs and trajectory similarity clustering is technically sound
Analytical proofs for truthfulness, individual rationality, and budget balance strengthen theoretical contribution
Three real-world datasets (DAIR-V2X, HighD, RCooper) provide credible experimental validation
Directly addresses a practically relevant problem in UAV-ground vehicle service matching
limitations: OSAA (one-step-ahead agreement) concept and binding mechanism assumptions need more scrutiny — what happens when vehicle deviates significantly?
Double auction truthfulness proof may rely on strong assumptions about incentive compatibility that aren't fully validated empirically
Mobility uncertainty hedging via preference lists is heuristic; lacks rigorous bound on worst-case regret
Privacy-accuracy tradeoff described qualitatively but not systematically parameterized
Clustering granularity for trajectory similarity not discussed — could be a key sensitivity parameter
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Three real-world datasets (DAIR-V2X, HighD, RCooper) is a strong base; however, simulation parameters (number of UAVs, vehicle density, privacy budget ranges) not fully specified, making reproducibility uncertain.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Strong theoretical contribution with novel LOSA framework addressing a real AGINs problem. Privacy-aware look-ahead is the key innovation. Analytical guarantees and three real-world datasets boost confidence. Minor concerns on OSAA enforceability and privacy budget sensitivity but overall solid work with high practical relevance.

overflow_list.title: Early Exiting U-Net for Efficient Processing on UAVs: A Case Study in Environmental Monitoring
overflow_list.authors: Luca Sartori Boni, Mohamed Moursi, Norbert Wehn, Bilal Hammoud
overflow_list.affiliations: Kaiserslautern-Landau, Germany | Union, or IoU). Despite this success, Tiny U-Net processes every | In this work, we combine these two lines of research and
overflow_list.abstract: Oil spills represent a severe threat, making early-stage thickness estimation crucial for guiding remediation efforts. Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are an attractive platform for environmental monitoring. However, due to their limited computation and power budgets, real-time onboard processing requires optimized algorithms or lightweight machine learning models. While the standard U-Net architecture is often too large for constrained UAV hardware, the compressed Tiny U-Net variant fits on FPGA platforms and achieves competitive estimation performance (0.79 in the metric Intersection over Union, or IoU). Despite this success, Tiny U-Net processes every radar image through the complete inference pipeline, resulting in unnecessary computation for simple cases. To address this inefficiency, we integrate an early exit feature into the Tiny U-Net architecture. We introduce an early exit branch that returns an early prediction when a compact confidence score exceeds a tunable threshold, bypassing deeper layers for high-confidence evaluations. Our experiments demonstrate that this design achieves comparable IoU to the full baseline model. Crucially, the technique is shown to reduce the average number of multiplications by up to 42% for an aggressive threshold, reducing the dynamic power consumption. Choosing a threshold that ensures extreme confidence reduces the complexity-reduction gains for an improved IoU. This early exit approach substantially improves computational efficiency in Tiny U-Net, enabling more practical deployment in UAV-based environmental monitoring systems.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 溢油构成严重威胁，早期厚度估计对指导修复至关重要。无人机（UAV）是环境监测的理想平台，但其计算和功耗有限，实时机载处理需优化算法或轻量级模型。标准U-Net对受限UAV硬件过大，压缩版Tiny U-Net适配FPGA且估计性能优异（IoU达0.79）。然而，Tiny U-Net对每张雷达图像均执行完整推理，在简单情况下造成冗余计算。为此，我们在Tiny U-Net中引入早退机制：当紧凑置信度分数超过可调阈值时，早退分支输出预测，跳过深层网络。实验表明，该设计IoU与完整基线相当，且在激进阈值下平均乘法次数减少高达42%，降低了动态功耗。选择确保极高置信度的阈值会降低复杂度削减收益，但能提升IoU。此早退方法大幅提升了Tiny U-Net的计算效率，使其在UAV环境监测系统中更具实用性。

strengths: Practical focus on UAV deployment constraints (power, compute)
Early exit is a well-motivated efficiency technique for edge devices
Real FPGA implementation context with Tiny U-Net baseline
Clear trade-off analysis between threshold aggression and accuracy
limitations: Early exiting is a well-established technique in efficient inference; application to U-Net is incremental
Limited dataset scope—single application domain (oil spill thickness)
No comparison to other efficient inference methods (neural architecture search, pruning, quantization in combination)
42% reduction is modest compared to state-of-the-art adaptive computation methods
Missing analysis of worst-case latency implications for safety-critical monitoring
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Appears to use limited proprietary or small-scale dataset; no mention of dataset size, diversity, or public benchmarks
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Competently executed but incremental contribution; limited scope and dataset weaken impact claims

overflow_list.title: A Variational Message Passing Framework for Multi-Sensor Multi-Object Tracking using Raw Radar Signals
overflow_list.authors: Anders Malthe Westerkam, Jakob Möderl, Erik Leitinger, Troels Pedersen
overflow_list.affiliations: Aalborg University, Aalborg Denmark | Graz University of Technology, Graz, Austria | tiongrant7538-1806andinpartby the Austrian Research Promoti on Agency | This paper advances the authors’ current line of research [42]
overflow_list.abstract: The growing proliferation of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) poses major challenges for reliable airspace surveillance, as drones are typically small, have low radar cross-sections, and often move slowly in cluttered environments. These characteristics make the joint tasks of detecting, localizing, and tracking multiple objects difficult for conventional detect-then-track (DTT) approaches, which rely on pre-processed measurements and may discard informative low-signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) signal components. To overcome these limitations, we propose a variational message passing (VMP)-based direct multiobject tracking (MOT) method that operates directly on raw radar signals and explicitly accounts for an unknown and time-varying number of objects. The proposed method is formulated for MIMO multi-radar systems and performs data fusion by jointly processing the signals of all radar sensors using a probabilistic model. A superimposed signal model is employed to capture correlations in the raw sensor data caused by closely spaced objects, and a hierarchical Bernoulli-Gamma model is introduced to jointly model object existence, reflectivities, and the reliability of individual radar-object links. Using a mean-field approximation, we derive message updates, yielding a computationally efficient VMP algorithm that simultaneously performs object detection, track formation, state estimation, and nuisance parameter learning directly from the radar signal. Simulation results in synthetic scenarios with weak and closely-spaced objects show that the proposed direct-MOT method outperforms a conventional pipeline based on super-resolution estimation followed by belief propagation (BP)-based tracking, particularly in low-SNR and clutter-rich conditions, demonstrating the advantages of direct signal-level inference and coherent multi-radar fusion.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 无人机普及为空域监视带来重大挑战，因其体积小、雷达截面积低且常在杂乱环境中低速移动。传统先检测后跟踪方法难以处理多目标检测、定位与跟踪任务，且可能丢失低信噪比信息成分。我们提出基于变分消息传递(VMP)的直接多目标跟踪方法，直接处理原始雷达信号并显式建模未知时变目标数。该方法面向MIMO多雷达系统，利用概率模型联合处理所有雷达信号。叠加信号模型捕捉密集目标数据相关性，层次Bernoulli-Gamma模型联合建模目标存在性、反射率及雷达-目标链路可靠性。利用平均场近似推导消息更新，得计算高效VMP算法，同时完成目标检测、航迹形成、状态估计及噪声参数学习。在弱目标和密集目标场景仿真中，所提直接MOT方法优于超分辨率估计加置信传播跟踪的常规流程，尤其在低信噪比和强杂波条件下，验证了直接信号级推理与相干多雷达融合的优势。

strengths: Novel direct-MOT paradigm that bypasses the traditional detect-then-track pipeline, operating on raw radar signals to preserve low-SNR information lost in pre-processing
Well-designed probabilistic framework: Bernoulli-Gamma hierarchical model jointly handles object existence, reflectivities, and radar-object link reliability
Superimposed signal model for closely-spaced objects is a meaningful contribution to the multi-radar fusion literature
Mean-field VMP derivation yields computationally tractable message updates while maintaining principled Bayesian inference
Demonstrated superiority over super-resolution + BP pipeline particularly in challenging low-SNR and clutter-rich regimes
limitations: Evaluation limited to synthetic scenarios—no real radar datasets (e.g., GOTCHA, MSTAR, or live drone radar data) are used
Computational scalability for real-time operation with many objects and large MIMO arrays is not discussed
The mean-field approximation may struggle with strong inter-object correlations that the superimposed model was designed to capture, creating a tension in the formulation
Radar-specific: applicability to RF sensing in 6G or integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) scenarios is not addressed
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Synthetic data only with designed challenging scenarios (low-SNR, closely-spaced objects, clutter). No real-world radar data validation. Baselines are well-chosen (super-resolution + BP).
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Strong methodological contribution with a novel raw-signal inference framework. Solid rigor and clear advantages over conventional pipelines. Held back by simulation-only validation and unaddressed scalability concerns.

overflow_list.summary_cn: 针对6G网络高数据流量需求，本文提出融合自主飞行器与多功能可重构智能表面（AM-RIS）的新架构，应用于流体天线辅助全双工网络。AM-RIS具备反射、放大和能量收集功能。通过联合优化波束成形、功率、AM-RIS配置及位置，采用混合深度强化学习框架（SOHRL）最大化能效。仿真验证方案优越性。

strengths: Novelous system architecture: integrating aerial MF-RIS with FA-assisted FD networks addresses a real 6G research gap
Well-motivated problem: EE maximization under complex hybrid discrete-continuous action space is practically relevant
Comprehensive joint optimization: beamforming, power, RIS config, and positioning are all considered together
Hybrid DRL framework (DQN+PPO) is architecturally sound for handling heterogeneous action spaces
Attention-driven state representation and meta-hyperparameter optimization add adaptive self-improvement capability
Clear and well-written abstract with logical structure and good English
Addresses sustainability via energy harvesting and EE awareness
limitations: SIMULATION ONLY evaluation — no real datasets, no real-world validation; this is a critical data quality weakness
No mathematical formulation visible in abstract — cannot assess theoretical rigor or optimality guarantees

Full-duplex BS, aerial platforms (drones), and MF-RIS are all immature/deployment-distant technologies — practical impact is speculative

The many design knobs (AM-RIS hybrid functions, FA positions, EH) may make the problem over-parameterized and hard to generalize
Baseline comparisons (no attention, standalone DRL variants) are expected but the specific performance claims lack statistical rigor without variance/confidence intervals in abstract

This is a significant limitation — the paper cannot demonstrate real-world viability, measurement-based accuracy, or deployment readiness. However, evaluation is purely simulation-based with no real datasets, and the abstract provides insufficient mathematical detail to assess theoretical rigor. The novelty is primarily in the system-level integration rather than fundamental algorithmic or theoretical breakthroughs. overflow_list.title: Joint Trajectory and Resource Optimization for Aerial RIS-assisted Integrated TNT Networks
overflow_list.authors: Vangara Saiprudhvi, Sandeep Singh, Keshav Singh, Hariharan Subramaniyam, Chih-Peng Li
overflow_list.affiliations: Institute | National University | Department School | School Institute
overflow_list.abstract: Integrated terrestrial and non-terrestrial networks (ITNTNs) are regarded as a key architectural paradigm for sixth-generation (6G) wireless systems. This paper investigates a dual-aerial reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted ITNTN, where a terrestrial base station (TBS) and a satellite (SAT) jointly serve terrestrial and satellite users with the aid of an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-mounted RIS and a high-altitude platform (HAP)-mounted RIS. We formulate an average sum-rate maximization problem by jointly optimizing the TBS and SAT precoders, the RIS phase shift matrices, and the three-dimensional trajectories of the UAV and the HAP, subject to transmit power, unit-modulus, and mobility constraints. The resulting optimization problem is highly non-convex due to the strong coupling among the transmit precoders, RIS phase shifts, and aerial platform mobility. To efficiently address this challenge, we propose a block coordinate descent (BCD) framework that integrates weighted minimum mean square error (WMMSE) optimization for precoder design, a manifold-based Riemannian conjugate gradient (RCG) method for RIS phase-shift optimization, and successive convex approximation (SCA) for trajectory optimization. The proposed algorithm is shown to converge to a stationary point. The simulation results show that the proposed joint design achieves an approximately $7.05 \%$ higher average sum-rate compared to the random RIS scheme, highlighting the effectiveness of dual-aerial RIS deployment and joint communication-mobility optimization in ITNTNs.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 本文研究双空中RIS辅助的天地一体化网络（ITNTN），地面基站与卫星在UAV-RIS和HAP-RIS协同下服务用户。构建平均和速率最大化问题，联合优化预编码器、RIS相移及飞行平台轨迹。提出块坐标下降框架，结合WMMSE、黎曼共轭梯度和逐次凸逼近方法。仿真显示联合设计相比随机RIS提升约7.05%。

strengths: Dual-aerial RIS (UAV + HAP) in an integrated terrestrial/non-terrestrial network is a reasonably novel system model combining two aerial tiers with satellite and terrestrial infrastructure
Well-structured optimization formulation with proper constraints (power, unit-modulus, mobility)
BCD framework with WMMSE + RCG + SCA is a methodical and sound decomposition; convergence to a stationary point is claimed
Clear and organized abstract; problem statement and methodology are well-articulated
limitations: The 7.05% gain over random RIS phase shifts is a very weak baseline — random is essentially a straw-man; comparison against optimized single-aerial RIS, fixed-position RIS, or state-of-the-art alternating optimization schemes is needed
Purely simulation-based evaluation with no real-world or even semi-empirical dataset; no channel measurement validation or trace-driven simulation
BCD + WMMSE + RCG + SCA is a highly standard recipe in the aerial RIS literature; the methodological contribution is incremental rather than innovative
HAP-mounted RIS is far from practical deployment; the system model is largely theoretical with unclear path to real-world implementation
Abstract does not discuss computational complexity, convergence speed, or scalability with more users/RIS elements — critical for aerial platforms with limited compute
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: No real-world data or channel measurements used. Evaluation is entirely simulation-based. The only reported quantitative result (7.05% over random RIS) uses an uncompetitive baseline, making it difficult to assess the true merit of the joint optimization. Missing: comparison against state-of-the-art optimized RIS baselines, convergence analysis, and complexity results.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: This paper follows a well-trodden path in the aerial RIS optimization literature: formulate a non-convex sum-rate problem, apply BCD decomposition with standard sub-problem solvers (WMMSE, RCG, SCA), and report modest gains over trivial baselines. The dual-aerial (UAV+HAP) system model adds some novelty, but the methodology is incremental and the experimental validation is weak. The 7.05% gain over random RIS is unconvincing. Likely a mid-tier journal or conference paper.

overflow_list.title: Joint Trajectory and Resource Optimization for Dual-aerial ARIS-assisted NOMA-TNT Networks
overflow_list.authors: Vangara Saiprudhvi, Keshav Singh, Hariharan Subramaniyam, Chih-Peng Li
overflow_list.affiliations: Institute | National University | Department School | School Institute
overflow_list.abstract: Integrated terrestrial and non-terrestrial networks (ITNTNs) are envisioned as a key paradigm for sixth-generation (6G) wireless systems, enabling seamless global connectivity. In this paper, we investigate a dual-aerial active reconfigurable intelligent surface (ARIS)-assisted non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA)-based ITNTN, where a terrestrial base station (TBS) and a satellite (SAT) simultaneously serve terrestrial and satellite users with the aid of a UAV-mounted ARIS and a HAP-mounted ARIS. Users are multiplexed via power-domain NOMA with a predefined SIC decoding order. We formulate an average sum-rate maximization problem by jointly optimizing transmit beamforming, ARIS coefficients, and the 3D trajectories of the UAV and HAP, subject to power, unit-modulus, ARIS power, and mobility constraints. The problem is highly non-convex due to coupled variables, nonlinear SINR expressions, ARIS amplification, and trajectory-dependent channels. To address this, a block coordinate descent (BCD)-based framework is proposed. Specifically, beamforming is optimized via WMMSE, ARIS phase shifts via a manifold-based RCG method, amplification factors via SCA, and trajectories via first-order approximations. The proposed algorithm is guaranteed to converge to a stationary point. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed design achieves significant performance gains over benchmark schemes. In particular, it provides an average sum-rate improvement of approximately $8.44\%$ over passive RIS under given power constraints, highlighting the benefits of dual-aerial ARIS and joint communication-mobility optimization.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 本文研究双空中有源RIS（ARIS）辅助的NOMA天地一体化网络，地面基站与卫星在UAV-ARIS和HAP-ARIS协同下服务用户。构建平均和速率最大化问题，联合优化波束成形、ARIS系数及轨迹。提出块坐标下降框架，采用WMMSE、黎曼共轭梯度和逐次凸逼近方法。仿真表明相比无源RIS提升约8.44%。

strengths: Combines multiple trending 6G topics (NOMA, ARIS, ITNTN, dual-aerial platforms) into a unified framework, which is ambitious in scope.
Proposes a BCD-based framework with dedicated sub-problem solvers (WMMSE, manifold-based RCG, SCA), which is methodologically sound for this class of problems.
Considers practical constraints including unit-modulus, ARIS power budgets, and 3D mobility constraints for both UAV and HAP.
limitations: The novelty is incremental: each individual component (NOMA, RIS optimization, UAV trajectory design, BCD) is well-established in existing literature; combining them is a packaging exercise rather than a methodological breakthrough.

The claimed 8.44% improvement over passive RIS is underwhelming — active RIS providing only single-digit percentage gains raises questions about whether the additional hardware complexity (amplifiers on aerial platforms) is justified.

No mention of real-world datasets, channel measurements, or experimental validation — appears to rely entirely on Monte Carlo simulations with standard channel models (e.g., Rayleigh/Rician), which limits practical credibility.
The problem formulation (sum-rate maximization with BCD) follows a highly templated structure seen in hundreds of RIS/UAV optimization papers, reducing the perceived contribution.

NOMA decoding with predefined SIC order in a dual-aerial ITNTN introduces significant fairness and practicality concerns that are not addressed (e.g., near-far effects across terrestrial-satellite users).
Satellite trajectory optimization is unrealistic — LEO/MEO satellite orbits are largely deterministic, not freely optimizable as implied.
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: No indication of real-world data or experimental validation. Likely relies on standard Monte Carlo simulations with Rayleigh/Rician fading models. Benchmark schemes are probably conventional baselines (passive RIS, no-RIS, orthogonal multiple access). The 8.44% improvement figure suggests modest gains. Without large-scale real-channel data or hardware prototype results, the evaluation is simulation-only and of limited practical说服力.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper is a systematic but incremental contribution in a crowded research area. The methodology (BCD+WMMSE+SCA+manifold) is standard and well-understood. The combination of dual-aerial ARIS with NOMA-TNTN is moderately novel but lacks a compelling practical motivation or experimental evidence. The 8.44% gain is a weak selling point for active RIS with significantly higher hardware cost. Likely suitable for a mid-tier IEEE journal but not a top venue (IEEE JSAC, TWC, or TCOM might accept with revisions; IEEE Access/TVT is more likely).

overflow_list.title: ESN-DAGMM: A Lightweight Framework for Unsupervised Time-Series Data Monitoring in 5G O-RAN Networks
overflow_list.authors: Andrew J Chen, Raymond Zhao, Lingjia Liu
overflow_list.abstract: Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) is an important 5G network architecture enabling flexible communication with adaptive strategies for different verticals. However, testing for O-RAN deployments involve massive volumes of time-series data (e.g., key performance indicators), creating critical challenges for scalable, unsupervised monitoring without labels or high computational overhead. To address this, we present ESN-DAGMM, a lightweight adaptation of the Deep Autoencoding Gaussian Mixture Model (DAGMM) framework for time series analysis. Our model utilizes an Echo State Network (ESN) to efficiently model temporal dependencies, proving effective in O-RAN networks where training samples are highly limited. Combined with DAGMM's integratation of dimensionality reduction and density estimation, we present a scalable framework for unsupervised monitoring of high volume network telemetry. When trained on only 10% of an O-RAN video-streaming dataset, ESN-DAGMM achieved on average 269.59% higher quality clustering than baselines under identical conditions, all while maintaining competitive reconstruction error. By extending DAGMM to capture temporal dynamics, ESN-DAGMM offers a practical solution for time-series analysis using very limited training samples, outperforming baselines and enabling operator's control over the clustering-reconstruction trade-off.
overflow_list.summary_cn: O-RAN部署测试涉及海量时序KPI数据，无监督监控面临可扩展性和计算开销挑战。本文提出ESN-DAGMM，结合回声状态网络(ESN)建模时序依赖与DAGMM的降维和密度估计，实现轻量级无监督监控。仅用10%训练样本，ESN-DAGMM聚类质量比基线平均提升269.59%，同时保持竞争力重构误差，为有限样本下的O-RAN时序分析提供实用方案。

strengths: Timely and practical problem: unsupervised monitoring of O-RAN KPIs is a real industry need
ESN as a lightweight temporal encoder is a sensible choice for limited-sample regimes — avoids full RNN training overhead
Extending DAGMM to temporal data is a logical and underexplored direction
10% training data claim is interesting and relevant for operator cost constraints
Scalability framing (high-volume telemetry) aligns well with O-RAN's architecture goals
limitations: Critical: no information about the O-RAN dataset — real vs simulated, scale (number of UEs/samples/timesteps), collection methodology, or whether it is publicly available
269.59% 'quality clustering' improvement is unexplained — which metric (NMI/AMI/DB index)? No absolute numbers provided, making the claim unverifiable and potentially misleading
No comparison against established unsupervised baselines: Isolation Forest, LOF, One-Class SVM, LSTM-AE, or standard DAGMM variants
DAGMM compression/estimation networks not described at all — impossible to assess architectural soundness or reproduce the method
No ablation study on ESN reservoir size, spectral radius, or sparsity — critical for reproducibility
No evaluation on publicly available time-series anomaly benchmarks (Yahoo S5, SMD, SWaT) — cross-domain credibility is missing
Reconstruction error reported but not anchored to any detection threshold methodology — no F1/AUROC/AUPR numbers
'Competitive reconstruction error' is vague — does not clarify whether competitive means better, equal, or slightly worse
No statistical significance testing reported across runs
The paper reads more as a system design poster than a rigorous research contribution — theory and derivation are essentially absent
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: The O-RAN video-streaming dataset is not described with sufficient detail: data source (simulation vs real deployment), scale, feature set, label availability, or public accessibility are all undisclosed. The 10% training split is mentioned but its rationale and impact on generalization are not evaluated. No external benchmark datasets are used for cross-validation, making it impossible to assess whether claims generalize beyond the authors' internal data. This is a significant red flag for a paper whose primary contribution is empirical.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The core idea of combining ESN (temporal encoder) with DAGMM (density estimation head) is a plausible and somewhat novel integration, and the O-RAN application domain is timely. However, the paper as presented reads like an early-stage work with critical gaps: no dataset description, unexplained metric, no established baselines, no architectural details, and no reproducibility path. A score of 46 reflects that the idea has merit but the execution is too incomplete for a strong assessment — more like a workshop paper than a journal/conference submission.

overflow_list.title: Learning Low-Dimensional Representation for O-RAN Testing via Transformer-ESN
overflow_list.authors: Jiongyu Dai, Raymond Zhao, Farhad Rezazadeh, Lizhong Zheng, Haining Wang, Lingjia Liu
overflow_list.abstract: Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) architectures enhance flexibility for 6G and NextG networks. However, it also brings significant challenges in O-RAN testing with evaluating abundant, high-dimensional key performance indicators (KPIs). In this paper, we introduce a novel two-stage framework to learn temporally-aware low-dimensional representations of O-RAN testing KPIs. To be specific, stage one employs an information-theoretic H-score to train a hybrid self-attentive transformer and echo state network (ESN) reservoir, called Transformer-ESN, capturing temporal dynamics and producing task-aligned $8$-dimensional embeddings. Stage two evaluates these embeddings by training a lightweight multilayer perceptron (MLP) predictor exclusively on them for key target KPIs such as reference signal received quality (RSRQ) and spectral efficiency. Using real-world O-RAN testbed data (video streaming with interference), our approach demonstrates a significant advantage specifically when training samples are very limited. In this scenario, the low-dimensional representations learned from the Transformer-ESN yield mean square error (MSE) reductions of up to 41.9\% for RSRQ and 29.9\% for spectral efficiency compared to predictions from the original high-dimensional data. The framework exhibits high efficiency for O-RAN testing, significantly reducing testing complexities for O-RAN systems.
overflow_list.summary_cn: O-RAN测试需评估高维KPI，本文提出两阶段框架学习时序感知低维表征：第一阶段用信息论H-score训练Transformer-ESN混合模型，生成8维任务对齐嵌入；第二阶段用轻量MLP预测RSRQ和频谱效率等目标KPI。基于真实O-RAN测试床数据，在训练样本极度有限时，低维表征使RSRQ的MSE降低41.9%、频谱效率MSE降低29.9%，显著降低O-RAN系统测试复杂度。

strengths: Novel combination of Transformer self-attention with ESN reservoir computing for temporal dynamics capture
Uses information-theoretic H-score for training embeddings, which is a principled approach
Addresses a real O-RAN testing challenge: high-dimensional KPI evaluation complexity
Two-stage architecture (representation learning + prediction) is conceptually sound
Strong practical motivation with clear industry relevance for 6G/NextG networks
Good presentation clarity with well-structured abstract

No baseline comparison with other representation learning methods (PCA, autoencoders, etc.)
Claim of 'real-world' testbed data lacks reproducibility details (vendor, deployment scenario)
No clear comparison against end-to-end deep learning approaches
MSE reduction percentages lack statistical significance (variance, confidence intervals)
Unclear what makes ESN essential vs. standard LSTM/GRU for temporal modeling
8-dimensional embedding choice not justified (ablation study missing)
Video streaming scenario may not generalize to other O-RAN use cases
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Claims real-world O-RAN testbed data but provides zero quantitative details: sample size, feature dimensionality, collection period, interference characteristics, and testbed setup are all omitted. Cannot verify if dataset scale supports the claims or if baselines are state-of-the-art.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Scored based on abstract alone. Strong motivation and novel architecture (Transformer-ESN) but data section is essentially empty - a real O-RAN testbed is claimed but no specifics given. Medium confidence because key technical details (why this architecture, H-score formulation, baseline methods) are missing from abstract.

overflow_list.title: Toward Environment-Aware LAE: SAR as a Shared Sensing Infrastructure
overflow_list.authors: Xue Zhang, Bang Huang, Mohamed-Slim Alouini
overflow_list.affiliations: Despite this rapid progress, the sensing foundation of | sensing modalities. It calls for a shared sensing foundation | SAR-Enabled Shared Sensing Foundation | foundation for the LAE | foundation is central to enabling scalable, reliable, and co
overflow_list.abstract: The rapid growth of the low-altitude economy (LAE) is making aerial systems an important part of future digital infrastructure. Although major advances have been achieved in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) platforms, communications, and autonomous control, environmental perception remains a key bottleneck to reliable and scalable LAE operations. Existing sensing modalities, such as optical, LiDAR, and millimeter-wave radar, are limited by visibility, sensing range, and environmental conditions, resulting in fragmented situational awareness. This article argues that addressing these limitations requires a shift from platform-centric sensing to a shared, environment-aware sensing infrastructure. In this context, synthetic aperture radar (SAR) offers a distinct advantage by enabling all-weather, wide-area perception. We show that SAR can support UAV operations through global environmental awareness, enhance task-level sensing, and enable cooperative sensing across satellites, high-altitude platforms, UAVs, and ground systems. Building on this perspective, we present a system-level view of SAR-enabled LAE, highlighting key transformations from fragmented to infrastructure-centric sensing, from reactive to predictive operation, and from device-centric to environment-aware networking. We further discuss enabling architectures, including multi-platform sensing hierarchies, integration with integrated sensing and communication (ISAC), and the role of artificial intelligence and digital twins, along with the key challenges toward real-world deployment. By positioning SAR as a shared sensing foundation rather than a standalone modality, this article provides new insights into the design of scalable, reliable, and intelligent LAE systems.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 低空经济（LAE）的快速发展使空中系统成为未来数字基础设施的重要组成。尽管无人机平台、通信和自主控制取得重大进展，环境感知仍是可靠且可扩展LAE运行的关键瓶颈。现有光学、激光雷达和毫米波雷达等感知方式受限于视距、感知范围和环境条件，导致态势感知碎片化。本文认为，需从以平台为中心的感知转向共享的环境感知基础设施。合成孔径雷达（SAR）具备全天候、广域感知优势，可通过全局环境感知支持无人机运行，增强任务级感知，并实现跨卫星、高空平台、无人机和地面系统的协同感知。基于此，我们提出SAR赋能LAE的系统级视图，强调从碎片化到基础设施中心、从反应式到预测式、从设备中心到环境感知网络的关键转变。我们还讨论了使能架构及实际部署挑战。将SAR定位为共享感知基础，为设计可扩展、可靠和智能的LAE系统提供了新见解。

strengths: Timely positioning of SAR for low-altitude economy infrastructure
Comprehensive system-level vision spanning satellites to ground
Integration with ISAC and digital twins aligns with 6G research trends
Addresses genuine gap in all-weather sensing for UAV operations
limitations: Position paper without technical contributions or validation
No experimental results, simulations, or system prototypes
Claims about SAR advantages lack quantitative backing (resolution, update rate, cost comparisons)
Vague on key challenges—no concrete technical obstacles or solutions proposed
Overlaps significantly with existing SAR-for-UAV survey literature
