quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Useful perspective piece but lacks technical depth and validation to be a strong research contribution; better suited for magazine or workshop

overflow_list.title: Efficient Transceiver Design for Aerial Image Transmission and Large-scale Scene Reconstruction
overflow_list.authors: Zeyi Ren, Jialin Dong, Wei Zuo, Yikun Wang, Bingyang Cheng, Sheng Zhou, Zhisheng Niu
overflow_list.abstract: Large-scale three-dimensional (3D) scene reconstruction in low-altitude intelligent networks (LAIN) demands highly efficient wireless image transmission. However, existing schemes struggle to balance severe pilot overhead with the transmission accuracy required to maintain reconstruction fidelity. To strike a balance between efficiency and reliability, this paper proposes a novel deep learning-based end-to-end (E2E) transceiver design that integrates 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) directly into the training process. By jointly optimizing the communication modules via the combined 3DGS rendering loss, our approach explicitly improves scene recovery quality. Furthermore, this task-driven framework enables the use of a sparse pilot scheme, significantly reducing transmission overhead while maintaining robust image recovery under low-altitude channel conditions. Extensive experiments on real-world aerial image datasets demonstrate that the proposed E2E design significantly outperforms existing baselines, delivering superior transmission performance and accurate 3D scene reconstructions.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 低空智能网络中大规模3D场景重建需高效无线图像传输。本文提出深度学习端到端收发机设计，将3D高斯泼溅（3DGS）融入训练过程，通过渲染损失联合优化通信模块。任务驱动框架支持稀疏导频方案，在降低传输开销的同时保持重建质量。实验表明该设计显著优于现有方案。

strengths: Integration of 3DGS rendering loss directly into E2E transceiver training is a creative task-driven design that aligns communication optimization with the downstream 3D reconstruction objective
Sparse pilot scheme enabled by the task-driven framework addresses a real overhead problem in aerial communication
Use of real-world aerial image datasets rather than purely synthetic data lends credibility
Clear and well-structured abstract that articulates the problem, gap, and contribution
limitations: E2E transceiver + task-oriented communication is an established paradigm; the novelty is primarily in the specific 3DGS integration rather than a fundamentally new formulation
Abstract provides no detail on channel model assumptions, MIMO configuration, or how 'low-altitude channel conditions' are modeled — rigor is hard to assess
Dataset scale, diversity, and baseline selection are unspecified; 'extensive experiments on real-world aerial image datasets' is a common but unverifiable claim at abstract level
No mention of computational complexity or inference latency of the 3DGS-augmented transceiver, which matters for real-time aerial deployment
Practical deployment gap remains large: lab-trained E2E transceivers with DL-based channel models rarely survive real-world fading and hardware impairments
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Abstract explicitly claims real-world aerial image datasets, which is a positive signal. However, no details on dataset scale, geographic diversity, or whether channel conditions are simulated or measured. Baseline comprehensiveness is also unclear. Without full paper verification, data quality appears adequate but not exceptional.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper sits at the intersection of task-oriented communication and 3D reconstruction, which is timely but builds on well-established E2E autoencoder paradigms. The 3DGS rendering loss as a training signal is a meaningful twist, but the core novelty is incremental. Data quality benefits from real-world images but channel modeling specifics are unknown. Practical impact is promising for UAV/surveying use cases but depends heavily on deployment robustness not demonstrated in the abstract.

overflow_list.title: Disaggregated multi-domain interference classification for O-RAN
overflow_list.authors: Dieter Verbruggen, Hazem Sallouha, Sofie Pollin
overflow_list.abstract: Spectrum sharing and dynamic spectrum reuse are becoming increasingly critical in modern wireless networks to address spectrum scarcity. However, these techniques inevitably increase Cross-Technology Interference (CTI). In this context, the Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN), as a modern and disaggregated network architecture, necessitates accurate, low-latency, and computationally efficient CTI classification and mitigation to support real-time control and maintain Quality of Service (QoS). Unfortunately, existing solutions predominantly rely on high-complexity, monolithic deep learning-based solutions that, while achieving high classification accuracy, incur significant latency and computational overhead This paper exploits the O-RAN functional split to leverage multi-domain raw signal representations (time, frequency, and Channel State Information (CSI)) directly from the same data stream. Each domain is processed locally, naturally interleaving CTI within the distributed, disaggregated O-RAN architecture. This distributed strategy enables a cost-aware, multi-domain fusion architecture that balances classification accuracy with computational overhead and latency. Our proposed multi-domain distributed architecture achieves a 400 $μs$ inference latency on standard CPUs. Compared to a state-of-the-art monolithic frequency-domain classifier, this represents an average 9x reduction in latency and an 11-fold decrease in computational cost, while sacrificing only 4% in classification performance and maintaining >90% accuracy in high-interference conditions.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 频谱共享增加跨技术干扰(CTI)，O-RAN架构需要低延迟高效率的CTI分类与缓解。现有方案依赖高复杂度单体深度学习，延迟和计算开销大。本文利用O-RAN功能切分，从同一数据流中提取时域、频域和CSI多域表征，在分布式架构中本地处理并融合，实现精度-开销平衡。所提架构在CPU上推理延迟400μs，比单体频域分类器延迟降低9倍、计算量减少11倍，仅损失4%分类精度，高干扰条件下仍保持>90%准确率。

strengths: Practical architectural alignment with O-RAN functional split — the distributed processing naturally maps to disaggregated RAN components, which is a sensible design choice
Impressive claimed latency reduction (9x) and computational cost savings (11x) while maintaining >90% accuracy, demonstrating a favorable accuracy-cost trade-off
Multi-domain fusion (time, frequency, CSI) from a single data stream is an efficient use of available information without requiring additional sensors
400μs inference on standard CPUs is practically relevant for real-time O-RAN xApp/rApp deployment, avoiding GPU dependency
limitations: Abstract mentions no specific datasets, evaluation scenarios, or interference types tested — unclear whether evaluation uses real-world RF captures or only synthetic simulations
No comparison against other distributed or lightweight approaches; the only baseline mentioned is a monolithic frequency-domain classifier, which is a relatively weak comparison point
4% accuracy sacrifice characterization lacks context — does this affect specific interference classes disproportionately? Class-level performance is critical for CTI mitigation
Cost-aware fusion mechanism is vague — no detail on how the cost model is defined or whether it adapts dynamically at runtime

Multi-domain signal extraction from the same data stream is claimed but not explained — time-frequency transforms and CSI estimation from a single stream raises questions about independence of features
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: The abstract provides zero information about the evaluation dataset. No mention of real-world RF measurements, synthetic channel models (e.g., 3GPP TR 38.901), interference technology types included, SNR ranges, or dataset scale. This is a significant red flag — CTI classification work heavily depends on the diversity and realism of interference scenarios, and purely synthetic evaluation would severely limit the paper's credibility. State-of-the-art baselines are also unclear.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper addresses a relevant O-RAN problem with a practical architectural approach and strong latency claims, but the complete absence of dataset details, limited baseline comparisons, and incremental novelty (multi-domain + distributed is not a new combination conceptually) cap the score. If the full paper reveals real-world RF datasets and comprehensive baselines, the score could rise to 60-65 range; if evaluation is simulation-only with narrow interference types, it could drop below 50.

overflow_list.title: Aerial IRS Deployment-Aided Secure Computation Offloading Against DISCO Jamming Attacks
overflow_list.authors: Minghui Min, Peng Zhang, Jiayang Xiao, Ruixin Yang, Shiyin Li, Huan Huang, Hongliang Zhang, Zhu Han
overflow_list.affiliations: China Universityof Miningand Technology,Xuzhou221116,China,andalso | College | University,Singapore639798.E-mail:minmh@cumt.edu.cn | China University School | School | University,China
overflow_list.abstract: With the rapid growth of Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC), secure and efficient computation offloading from user equipment (UEs) to edge access points (APs) is critical. However, DISCO intelligent reflective surface-based fully-passive jammers (DIRS-based FPJs) use random time-varying phase shifts to launch DISCO jamming attacks, disrupting offloading performance. This paper leverages an aerial intelligent reflective surface (AIRS) to enable secure computation offloading against DISCO jamming by jointly optimizing offloading ratios, AIRS phase shifts, and deployment. A two-timescale (2Ts) framework is proposed to address the optimization challenge caused by the distinct update frequencies of different strategies. Specifically, AIRS deployment is adjusted on a long timescale to boost antijamming capability due to the impracticality of frequent physical adjustment, while offloading ratios and phase shifts are optimized on a short timescale to adapt to DIRS-jammed dynamic channel conditions. We propose a dual-agent deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based AIRS deployment-aided secure computation offloading (DDADSO) scheme to maximize the secure offloading utility under DISCO jamming. Simulation results verify that the proposed DDADSO scheme outperforms benchmark schemes, demonstrating the effectiveness of AIRS deployment in improving offloading performance against DISCO jamming attacks.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 针对MEC中的DISCO智能反射面干扰攻击问题，本文利用空中智能反射面（AIRS）实现安全计算卸载。提出双时间尺度框架：长时尺调整AIRS部署，短时尺优化卸载比例和相移。设计双智能体深度强化学习方案（DDADSO），联合优化策略以最大化安全卸载效用。仿真表明该方案有效抵御DISCO干扰。

strengths: Addresses a timely and relevant problem: securing MEC computation offloading against intelligent jamming (DISCO), which is an emerging threat model beyond conventional jamming.

The two-timescale (2Ts) framework is well-motivated — physically adjusting IRS deployment is inherently slow, while phase shifts and offloading ratios can adapt faster. This separation is practically sound.
Jointly optimizing three interdependent variables (offloading ratios, AIRS phase shifts, and AIRS deployment position) captures the core trade-off space of the problem.
The use of aerial IRS (AIRS) rather than fixed IRS adds deployment flexibility, which is a meaningful extension for dynamic MEC scenarios.
limitations: The problem formulation and solution methodology closely follow the now-standard IRS + DRL optimization template. Many prior works already use DRL for IRS phase shift optimization, and two-timescale frameworks are well-established in the IRS literature. The incremental contribution is mainly in the specific threat model (DISCO jamming).

Dual-agent DRL introduces significant training complexity and hyperparameter sensitivity. The abstract provides no discussion of convergence, training overhead, or stability — critical concerns for DRL-based schemes in dynamic environments.

No analytical characterization of the DISCO jamming model's impact on offloading performance. A rigorous information-theoretic or game-theoretic baseline analysis would strengthen the paper significantly.
The scalability of the proposed scheme is unclear. As the number of UEs, DIRS-based jammers, or IRS elements increases, the joint action space grows combinatorially, which may render DRL impractical.
Only 'simulation results' are mentioned — no real-world testbed validation or even system-level simulation (e.g., using ns-3 or a channel emulator).
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Purely simulation-based evaluation with no real-world datasets or system-level simulators. The DISCO jamming channel model is likely analytically simplified. No mention of dataset scale, number of evaluation scenarios, or statistical significance testing. Benchmark schemes are not specified in the abstract.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper addresses a relevant intersection of MEC security, IRS, and adversarial jamming, but the contribution is largely incremental — combining existing IRS optimization techniques (DRL + two-timescale) with a specific jamming model. The absence of real-world validation, analytical depth, and clarity on DRL scalability limits its impact. A mid-tier conference (e.g., IEEE GLOBECOM, ICC, or VTC) is a likely venue.

overflow_list.title: GreenScatter: Through-Canopy Soil Moisture Sensing with UAV-Mounted Radar
overflow_list.authors: Luke Jacobs, Ishfaq Aziz, Benhao Lu, Alireza Tabatabaeenejad, Mohamad Alipour, Elahe Soltanaghai
overflow_list.abstract: Soil moisture is a critical variable for managing irrigation, improving crop yield, and understanding field-scale hydrology. Radars mounted on unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) offer a promising means to monitor soil moisture over large fields with flexible, high-resolution coverage. However, during the growing season, canopy scattering and soil reflections become strongly coupled in the radar measurement. These coupled effects vary with crop structure or flight altitude, complicating the retrieval of soil moisture. To overcome this challenge, we present GreenScatter, a physics-based soil moisture retrieval framework for nadir-looking wideband UAV radars. GreenScatter introduces a microwave radiative transfer model that explicitly captures the dominant electromagnetic interactions between vegetation and soil, enabling accurate modeling of coherent ground backscatter through canopy. In parallel, it develops a radar cross-section (RCS) estimation method that transforms time-domain radar signals into calibrated wideband RCS spectra, isolating soil reflections while compensating for hardware and waveform effects. Together, these components enable robust soil moisture estimation through vegetation across varying canopy conditions and UAV configurations. Field experiments across multiple corn and soybean sites demonstrate consistent retrieval with an average volumetric water content (VWC) error of 4.49%.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 土壤水分是管理灌溉、提高作物产量和理解田间水文的关键变量。无人机雷达为大面积农田土壤水分监测提供了灵活高分辨率的手段。但在生长季，冠层散射与土壤反射在雷达测量中强烈耦合，且随作物结构或飞行高度变化，增加了土壤水分反演的难度。为此，我们提出GreenScatter，一种针对天底视宽带无人机雷达的基于物理的土壤水分反演框架。该框架引入微波辐射传输模型，显式捕捉植被与土壤间的主要电磁相互作用，实现对穿透冠层相干地面后向散射的精确建模；同时开发雷达截面积（RCS）估计方法，将时域雷达信号转换为校准的宽带RCS谱，隔离土壤反射并补偿硬件与波形影响。两者结合实现了不同冠层条件和无人机配置下穿透植被的稳健土壤水分估计。多玉米和大豆田间的实验表明反演结果一致，平均体积含水量误差为4.49%。

strengths: Novel physics-based approach to through-canopy soil moisture retrieval—addresses hard inverse problem
Well-developed radiative transfer model with explicit vegetation-soil interaction modeling
Calibrated RCS estimation pipeline with hardware/waveform compensation
Real field validation across multiple crop types (corn and soybean) and sites
Practical significance for precision agriculture with concrete error metric (4.49% VWC)
limitations: Limited to nadir-looking geometry; oblique angles not addressed
Single radar platform; multi-UAV coordination for larger coverage not explored
No real-time processing analysis or onboard implementation constraints
Comparison to existing retrieval methods (e.g., SMAP, Sentinel-1 based) limited or absent
Seasonal/generalization analysis across full growing cycle not detailed
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Real field experiments across multiple sites and crop types; appears to be actual UAV radar measurements though dataset size and temporal span not fully specified
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Strong technical contribution with physics-based innovation and real validation; highly relevant to precision agriculture and UAV remote sensing

overflow_list.title: Plasticity-Enhanced Multi-Agent Mixture of Experts for Dynamic Objective Adaptation in UAVs-Assisted Emergency Communication Networks
overflow_list.authors: Wen Qiu, Zhiqiang He, Wei Zhao, Hiroshi Masui
overflow_list.affiliations: Department of Information and Communication | Engineering, Kitami Institute of Technology, Japan | dynamics [15], [16]. (3) Meta RL learns priors or update | gineering, the University of Electro-Communications, Japan | School of Computer Science and Technology, Anhui | University of Technology, China
overflow_list.abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles serving as aerial base stations can rapidly restore connectivity after disasters, yet abrupt changes in user mobility and traffic demands shift the quality of service trade-offs and induce strong non-stationarity. Deep reinforcement learning policies suffer from plasticity loss under such shifts, as representation collapse and neuron dormancy impair adaptation. We propose plasticity enhanced multi-agent mixture of experts (PE-MAMoE), a centralized training with decentralized execution framework built on multi-agent proximal policy optimization. PE-MAMoE equips each UAV with a sparsely gated mixture of experts actor whose router selects a single specialist per step. A non-parametric Phase Controller injects brief, expert-only stochastic perturbations after phase switches, resets the action log-standard-deviation, anneals entropy and learning rate, and schedules the router temperature, all to re-plasticize the policy without destabilizing safe behaviors. We derive a dynamic regret bound showing the tracking error scales with both environment variation and cumulative noise energy. In a phase-driven simulator with mobile users and 3GPP-style channels, PE-MAMoE improves normalized interquartile mean return by 26.3\% over the best baseline, increases served-user capacity by 12.8\%, and reduces collisions by approximately 75\%. Diagnostics confirm persistently higher expert feature rank and periodic dormant-neuron recovery at regime switches.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 作为空中基站的无人机能在灾后快速恢复连接，但用户移动性和流量需求的突变会改变服务质量的权衡并引发强烈的非平稳性。深度强化学习策略在此类变化下会遭受可塑性损失，表征崩溃和神经元休眠损害了适应能力。我们提出可塑性增强的多智能体混合专家（PE-MAMoE），基于多智能体近端策略优化的集中训练分散执行框架。PE-MAMoE为每架无人机配备稀疏门控混合专家执行器，路由器每步选择一个专家。非参数相位控制器在相位切换后注入短暂的专家随机扰动，重置动作对数标准差，退火熵和学习率，并调度路由器温度，以在不破坏安全行为的情况下重塑策略可塑性。我们推导了动态遗憾界，表明跟踪误差随环境变化和累积噪声能量缩放。在具有移动用户和3GPP信道的相位驱动模拟器中，PE-MAMoE将归一化四分位平均回报提升26.3%，服务用户容量提升12.8%，碰撞减少约75%。诊断证实了专家特征秩持续较高及在状态切换时休眠神经元的周期性恢复。

strengths: Novel combination of plasticity enhancement with Mixture of Experts in multi-agent RL—addresses understudied problem of non-stationarity in UAV networks
Well-motivated biological inspiration (plasticity loss, dormant neurons) with diagnostic validation
Strong theoretical contribution with dynamic regret bound derivation
Comprehensive evaluation: 26.3% return improvement, 12.8% capacity increase, 75% collision reduction
Phase controller design is practical and implementable for real UAV systems
limitations: Simulation-based evaluation only; no real UAV flight tests or hardware-in-the-loop validation
3GPP-style channels are simplified; real disaster scenarios have more complex propagation
CTDE framework requires central coordination which may be unreliable post-disaster
Computational overhead of MoE routing and phase control not quantified for resource-constrained UAVs
Comparison to simpler plasticity methods (reset-only, replay-based) could be stronger
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Custom phase-driven simulator with mobile users and 3GPP-style channels; no real-world disaster mobility traces or field deployment data
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Strong technical innovation at intersection of RL theory and wireless networking; simulation limits are acceptable given problem domain and theoretical rigor

overflow_list.title: Generative AI Agent Empowered Power Allocation for HAP Propulsion and Communication Systems
overflow_list.authors: Xiaoyu Xing, Dingyi Lu, Peng Yang, Zehui Xiong, Xianbin Cao, Tony Q. S. Quek
overflow_list.affiliations: HAP communication power allocation: Much research | partby the Fundamental Research Fundsfor the Central Universities,andin | mit power of HAP terminals, subject to the constraints on | part by the National Research Foundation, Singapore and Infocomm Media | IEEE/CICInternational Conference on Communications in China[DOI | In [11], the authors proposed a joint user association and
overflow_list.abstract: High altitude platforms (HAPs) are emerging as a key enabler for 6G coverage, yet limited energy must be split between propulsion and communications. Most prior HAP studies ignore propulsion power or rely on surrogates that miss hull-propeller interference, leading to misestimated communication power budgets and degraded beamforming. More importantly, HAP power allocation is intrinsically a multi-system, multidisciplinary problem in which aerodynamics, propulsion-system efficiency, and communication-system performance (quality of service (QoS) and energy efficiency (EE)) are tightly coupled.To address these challenges, this paper designs an interactive generative artificial intelligence (AI)-empowered HAP power allocation agent.By interacting with the AI agent, we develop an accurate propulsion power consumption model that takes into account the aerodynamic interference between the HAP's hull and the propeller. Assisted by the AI agent, we further formulate a HAP beamforming problem to improve user QoS and enhance the EE of the HAP communication system.This paper also proposes a QoS-enhanced energy-efficient (Q3E) beamforming algorithm to solve the formulated problem.Simulation results demonstrate the accuracy of the propulsion-power model and the effectiveness of the Q3E algorithm.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 高空平台(HAP)是6G覆盖的关键使能技术，但有限能量需在推进与通信间分配。现有HAP研究多忽略推进功率或采用未考虑机身-螺旋桨气动干扰的替代模型，导致通信功率预算估算错误、波束赋形性能下降。更重要的是，HAP功率分配本质上是涉及气动学、推进系统效率与通信系统性能（服务质量与能源效率）的多系统多学科问题，各方面紧密耦合。为解决这些挑战，本文设计基于交互式生成式AI赋能的HAP功率分配智能体。通过与AI智能体交互，我们开发了计及机身与螺旋桨气动干扰的精确推进功率消耗模型。在AI智能体辅助下，我们进一步构建HAP波束赋形问题以提升用户服务质量并增强通信系统能源效率。本文还提出服务质量增强的能源高效(Q3E)波束赋形算法。仿真结果验证了推进功率模型的准确性及Q3E算法的有效性。

strengths: Addresses a genuinely multidisciplinary and underexplored problem: the tight coupling between HAP aerodynamics, propulsion, and communication power budgets in 6G scenarios
Propulsion power model accounting for hull-propeller interference is a practical engineering contribution that prior surrogate-based approaches miss
The Q3E beamforming algorithm targeting joint QoS and energy efficiency is well-motivated for energy-constrained HAP platforms
limitations: The 'interactive generative AI agent' methodology is poorly defined—unclear whether this refers to LLM-based interaction, reinforcement learning, or another paradigm, raising reproducibility concerns
Simulation-only evaluation with no discussion of fidelity or validation against real HAP propulsion data
The abstract is verbose and structurally loose; key algorithmic details of Q3E are absent, making independent assessment difficult
No comparison with established optimization baselines (e.g., convex relaxation, sequential convex programming, or standard multi-objective optimization) for the beamforming problem
Propulsion power modeling is inherently platform-specific; generalizability across different HAP designs is not discussed
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Simulation-only with no real HAP data. No baseline algorithms reported. Propulsion model validation details absent.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Interesting multidisciplinary problem formulation for 6G HAPs, but vague AI agent methodology, limited rigor in presentation, and weak empirical validation lower the overall score significantly.

overflow_list.title: FORSLICE: An Automated Formal Framework for Efficient PRB-Allocation towards Slicing Multiple Network Services
overflow_list.authors: Debarpita Banerjee, Sumana Ghosh, Snigdha Das, Shilpa Budhkar, Rana Pratap Sircar
overflow_list.abstract:
Network slicing is a modern 5G technology that provides efficient network experience for diverse use cases. It is a technique for partitioning a single physical network infrastructure into multiple virtual networks, called slices, each equipped for specific services and requirements. In this work, we particularly deal with radio access network (RAN) slicing and resource allocation to RAN slices. In 5G, physical resource blocks (PRBs) being the fundamental units of radio resources, our main focus is to allocate PRBs to the slices efficiently. While addressing a spectrum of needs for multiple services or the same services with multi-priorities, we need to ensure two vital system properties: i) fairness to every service type (i.e., providing the required resources and a desired range of throughput) even after prioritizing a particular service type, and ii) PRB-optimality or minimizing the unused PRBs in slices. These serve as the core performance evaluation metrics for PRB-allocation in our work.
  We adopt the 3-layered hierarchical PRB-partitioning technique for allocating PRBs to network slices. The case-specific, AI-based solution of the state-of-the-art method lacks sufficient correctness to ensure consistent system performance. To achieve guaranteed correctness and completeness, we leverage formal methods and propose the first approach for a fair and optimal PRB distribution to RAN slices. We formally model the PRB-allocation problem as a 3-layered framework, FORSLICE, specifically by employing satisfiability modulo theories. Next, we apply formal verification to ensure that the desired system properties: fairness and PRB-optimality, are satisfied by the model. The proposed method offers an efficient, versatile and automated approach compatible with all 3-layered hierarchical network structure configurations, yielding significant system property improvements compared to the baseline.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 针对5G RAN切片的PRB分配问题，提出首个基于形式化方法（可满足性模理论）的公平最优分配框架FORSLICE，确保资源分配的公平性和利用率，为三层分层网络结构提供高效自动化方案。

strengths: First formal-methods-based approach for PRB allocation in RAN slicing — genuine methodological novelty vs. dominant AI/heuristic paradigm
SMT-based modeling provides correctness and completeness guarantees that AI/ML approaches fundamentally cannot offer
Dual property verification (fairness + PRB-optimality) is well-motivated and cleanly formulated
Compatible with all 3-layered hierarchical configurations, offering versatility
limitations: Abstract mentions only a single 'baseline' comparison — no indication of comprehensive benchmarking against state-of-the-art AI/optimization methods
No mention of real-world dataset or large-scale simulation; formal methods papers often evaluate on small synthetic scenarios, raising scalability concerns
SMT solver runtime is unaddressed — PRB allocation in live RAN operates at sub-millisecond timescales; solver overhead may be prohibitive for real-time deployment
The 3-layered hierarchical structure is adopted from prior work; the core allocation logic within each layer lacks detail in the abstract
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Abstract provides no evidence of real-world or large-scale simulation data. Mentions only 'significant improvements compared to the baseline' without specifying dataset, scale, or baseline identity. Likely relies on small synthetic/formal verification case studies, which is insufficient for validating practical RAN slicing performance claims.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper's primary contribution is replacing AI-based heuristics with SMT-formalized guarantees — a legitimate and well-motivated methodological shift. However, formal methods approaches in wireless resource allocation consistently struggle with scalability and real-time feasibility, and the abstract gives no indication the authors have addressed this gap. The absence of any mention of real data, simulation scale, or solver runtime performance significantly drags down the data quality and practical impact scores.

overflow_list.title: Quality-Aware Denoising of Ultra-Short TDoA Measurements for 5G-NR UAV Localization
overflow_list.authors: Zexin Fang, Bin Han, Anjie Qiu, Zhuojun Tian, Hans D. Schotten
overflow_list.affiliations: RPTU University Kaiserslautern-Landau, Germany | KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Sweden | German Research Center for Artificial | Intelligence (DFKI), Germany | China Mobile and China Unicom in major cities, Verizon and | Vodafone trials in Europe. Despite these advances, achieving
overflow_list.abstract: Reliable positioning is essential for Uncrewed Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) in safety-critical urban operations, yet achieving sub-meter accuracy under stringent latency constraints remains challenging. While 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) specifies repeated Positioning Reference Signals (PRS) transmissions for accurate Time Difference of Arrival (TDoA) measurements, denoising techniques specifically tailored for extremely limited measurement sequences within 3GPP frameworks remain underexplored. We propose Adaptive Gain Exponential Smoother (AGES), a lightweight filter combining exponentially weighted averaging with adaptive gains informed by 3GPP measurement quality reports. Simulations demonstrate AGES achieves 30-40% reduction in positioning error with only 3-5 repeated measurements while maintaining Fifth Generation New Radio (5G-NR) infrastructure compatibility.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 可信赖定位对无人机安全关键城市作业至关重要，但在严格延迟约束下实现亚米级精度仍具挑战。3GPP规定了重复定位参考信号以实现精确到达时差测量，但针对3GPP框架内极有限测量序列的去噪技术仍待探索。我们提出自适应增益指数平滑器(AGES)，一种结合指数加权平均与3GPP测量质量报告自适应增益的轻量滤波器。仿真表明AGES仅需3-5次重复测量即可降低30-40%定位误差，同时保持5G-NR基础设施兼容性。

strengths: Directly addresses a practical gap in 3GPP PRS-based localization: denoising ultra-short measurement sequences under strict latency constraints
Claims 30-40% positioning error reduction with only 3-5 repeated measurements, which is valuable for time-critical UAV operations
Leverages existing 3GPP measurement quality reports (e.g., RSRP-based indicators) as adaptive gain inputs, maintaining backward compatibility with 5G-NR infrastructure
limitations: Exponential smoothing with adaptive gains is a well-established technique; the core algorithmic contribution is incremental rather than fundamental
Evaluation relies entirely on simulations—no real-world 5G-NR measurement data, hardware testbed, or field trial results are presented
The abstract does not specify the simulation environment (channel model, UE density, NLOS conditions), making it difficult to assess scenario realism
TDoA-only approach ignores complementary positioning methods (AoA, multi-RTT) that 3GPP Release 17+ supports jointly
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Simulation-only evaluation with no reported real dataset. Scale and baseline details not provided in the abstract.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Solid practical motivation and 3GPP alignment, but limited novelty (adaptive exponential smoothing) and lack of empirical validation cap the score.

overflow_list.title: LITE: Lightweight Channel Gain Estimation with Reduced X-Haul CSI Signaling in O-RAN
overflow_list.authors: David Goez, Marco Piazzola, Giulia Costa, Achiel Colpaert, Rodney Martinez Alonso, Esra Aycan Beyazit, Nina Slamnik-Krijestorac, Johann M. Marquez-Barja, Miguel Camelo Botero
overflow_list.abstract: Cell-Free Massive Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (CF-MaMIMO) in Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) promises high spectral efficiency but is limited by frequent Channel State Information (CSI) exchanges, which strain fronthaul/midhaul/backhaul (X-haul) bandwidth and exceed the capabilities of existing approaches relying on uncompressed CSI or heavy predictors. To overcome these constraints, we propose LITE, a lightweight pipeline combining a 1-D convolutional Autoencoder (AE) at the O-RAN Distributed Unit (O-DU) with a Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE)-enhanced Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) predictor at the Near-Real-Time RAN Intelligent Controller (Near-RT-RIC), enabling short-horizon trajectory-unaware forecasting under strict transport and processing budgets. LITE applies 50% CSI compression and an asymmetric SE-BiLSTM, reducing model complexity by 83.39% while improving accuracy by 5% relative to a baseline BiLSTM. With compression-aware training, the Lightweight Intelligent Trajectory Estimator (LITE) incurs only 6% accuracy loss versus the BiLSTM baseline, outperforming independent and end-to-end strategies. A TensorRT-optimized implementation achieves 147k Queries per Second (QPS), a 4.6x throughput gain. These results demonstrate that LITE delivers X-haul-efficient, low-latency, and deployment-ready channel-gain prediction compatible with O-RAN splits.
overflow_list.summary_cn: O-RAN中无小区大规模MIMO受限于频繁CSI交换对X-haul带宽的压力。本文提出LITE轻量级流水线：O-DU端1-D卷积自编码器压缩CSI，Near-RT-RIC端SE增强BiLSTM预测信道增益。LITE实现50%CSI压缩，模型复杂度降低83.39%，精度提升5%；压缩感知训练仅6%精度损失，优于独立和端到端策略。TensorRT优化实现147k QPS，吞吐提升4.6倍，满足O-RAN切分下X-haul高效低延迟部署需求。

strengths: Addresses practical O-RAN deployment constraints with X-haul bandwidth optimization
Impressive efficiency gains: 83.39% complexity reduction with 5% accuracy improvement
Compression-aware training shows thoughtful integration of prediction and compression tasks
TensorRT optimization and 147k QPS demonstrates deployment readiness
Clear problem formulation for CF-MaMIMO in O-RAN architecture
limitations: No real-world CSI dataset or field trial mentioned - relies on simulation/synthetic data
6% accuracy loss after compression may be problematic for practical MIMO systems requiring high channel estimation precision
Short-horizon prediction limits applicability for proactive resource allocation
Comparison to only BiLSTM baseline is narrow - lacks comparison to state-of-the-art CSI prediction methods
Novelty is incremental: Autoencoder compression and BiLSTM prediction are established techniques
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Abstract does not mention any real-world dataset, field measurements, or testbed experiments. Appears to rely entirely on synthetic/simulated CSI data, which significantly undermines practical validity claims for O-RAN deployment scenarios.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper addresses an important practical problem in O-RAN deployments and achieves impressive efficiency metrics. However, the lack of real-world data validation is a critical weakness that undermines the deployment-ready claims. Novelty is moderate (combination of existing techniques). Score reflects strong engineering contribution but limited research advancement and unvalidated practical applicability.

overflow_list.title: RieIF: Knowledge-Driven Riemannian Information Flow for Robust Spatio-Temporal Graph Signal Prediction in 6G Wireless Networks
overflow_list.authors: Zhonghao Jiu, Yongming Huang, Fan Meng, Hang Zhan, Zening Liu, Xiaohu You
overflow_list.abstract: With 6G evolving towards intelligent network autonomy, artificial intelligence (AI)-native operations are becoming pivotal. Wireless networks continuously generate rich and heterogeneous data, which inherently exhibits spatio-temporal graph structure. However, limited radio resources result in incomplete and noisy network measurements. This challenge is further intensified when a target variable and its strongest correlates are missing over contiguous intervals, forming systemic blind spots. To tackle this issue, we propose RieIF (Knowledge-driven Riemannian Information Flow), a geometry-consistent framework that incorporates knowledge graphs (KGs) for robust spatio-temporal graph signal prediction. For analytical tractability within the Fisher-Rao geometry, we project the input from a Riemannian manifold onto a positive unit hypersphere, where angular similarity is computationally efficient. This projection is implemented via a graph transformer, using the KG as a structural prior to constrain attention and generate a micro stream. Simultaneously, a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model captures temporal dynamics to produce a macro stream. Finally, the micro stream (highlighting geometric shape) and the macro stream (emphasizing signal strength) are adaptively fused through a geometric gating mechanism for signal recovery. Experiments on three wireless datasets show consistent improvements under systemic blind spots, including up to 31% reduction in root mean squared error and up to 3.2 dB gain in recovery signal-to-noise ratio, while maintaining robustness to graph sparsity and measurement noise.
overflow_list.summary_cn: RieIF：基于知识图谱与黎曼几何的无线网络时空图信号恢复框架。将黎曼流形投影至正定单位超球面，通过图Transformer结合知识先验与LSTM分别提取几何特征与时序动态，实验在系统盲区场景下均方根误差降低31%，恢复信噪比提升3.2dB。

strengths: Strong 6G network AI motivation — tackles a real and timely problem: incomplete/noisy measurements with systemic blind spots
Creative architectural fusion: Fisher-Rao geometry + knowledge graph + graph transformer + LSTM — non-trivial integration that is not just a vanilla GNN baseline
Micro-macro stream decomposition with geometric gating is architecturally distinctive and conceptually sound
Solid performance claims: 31% RMSE reduction and 3.2 dB SNR gain across three datasets — concrete and credible
Addresses graph sparsity and noise robustness explicitly, which are practical concerns in real wireless deployments
limitations: Dataset details entirely absent from abstract — no dataset names, scale, collection methodology, or real-vs-synthetic designation; major red flag for data quality assessment
Baseline comparison is vague ('consistent improvements') — no comparison against state-of-the-art methods or ablation of individual components provided
Mathematical depth is impossible to verify from abstract alone — Fisher-Rao projection details, attention formulation, and gating mechanism need scrutiny
Knowledge graph construction and its integration mechanism are under-described — critical for reproducibility
No complexity/real-time feasibility discussion — 6G deployment requires computational budget analysis, which is missing
Angular similarity on hypersphere for Fisher-Rao geometry is a known approach in some domains (e.g., brain connectomics); novelty may be partially incremental over prior Riemannian deep learning work
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Three wireless datasets are claimed but not named, characterized (real vs synthetic), or sized. Performance metrics are specific (31% RMSE reduction, 3.2 dB SNR gain) which adds credibility, but without dataset identity or data scale, reproducibility and generalizability cannot be assessed. This is the most significant gap in the submission.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Scores are assigned with medium confidence. The paper presents a well-motivated and architecturally novel framework for 6G spatio-temporal prediction, with plausible mathematical scaffolding (Fisher-Rao geometry) and credible performance gains. However, the data quality is unverifiable from the abstract alone (no dataset names or scale), technical rigor details are hidden behind the abstraction, and baseline comparisons are insufficiently described. If the datasets are real and the baselines are truly state-of-the-art (e.g., Graph WaveNet, AGCRN), the score would increase to ~72; if datasets are synthetic or small-scale, it would drop to ~52. The overall 62 reflects a competent but incomplete submission that likely needs major revision before acceptance at a top venue.

overflow_list.title: Enhancing Secure Intent-Based Networking with an Agentic AI: The EU Project MARE Approach
overflow_list.authors: Iulisloi Zacarias, Marla Grunewald, Fin Gentzen, Xavi Masip-Bruin, Admela Jukan
overflow_list.abstract: In the EU project MARE, a novel plane was proposed and used in combination with intent-based networking (IBN), allowing the operator to focus on what, rather than on how. Recently, LLMs have been successfully employed to translate the high-level intents into low-level actions. The open challenge is to understand how IBN can be effectively enhanced with LLM and the emerging agentic AI for security purposes. Enhancing IBN with an agentic AI paradigm introduces significant challenges that existing solutions do not fully address. This paper proposes an enhanced IBN framework with a strong security focus toward agentic AI. We address the architectural and security requirements for a multi-agent intent-based system (IBS) architecture, including a multi-domain IBN. We propose a hierarchical multi-agent and multi-vendor architecture that can also be applied more broadly in 6G architectures and beyond, beyond the security architecture proposed in MARE. The architecture incorporates an interactive intent-processing pipeline using LLMs, and it also allows the IBS to connect to external security knowledge bases, such as MITRE ATT\&CK, MITRE FiGHT, and NIST.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 提出面向安全意图驱动的多智能体增强框架，结合LLM实现高层意图转底层操作，设计跨域多层次架构，支持接入MITRE ATT&CK等安全知识库，为6G安全架构提供新思路。

strengths: Addresses timely intersection of IBN, LLM, and agentic AI for security
Proposes hierarchical multi-agent architecture applicable to 6G
Integrates external security knowledge bases (MITRE ATT&CK, NIST)
EU project backing provides credibility and potential resources
limitations: Abstract lacks any empirical validation or evaluation results
No mention of real testbed, dataset, or prototype implementation
Security claims are architectural only without threat modeling or attack evaluation
Vague on how LLM intent translation handles ambiguity or adversarial inputs
No comparison with existing IBN security solutions
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Abstract mentions no experiments, datasets, simulations, or even preliminary results. Claims are purely architectural/conceptual. Score 30 reflects complete absence of empirical data—cannot assess validity of security enhancements without evaluation.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Position paper proposing architecture without validation. May get workshop acceptance but unlikely for top-tier venue without experimental results. Score 52 reflects conceptual promise undermined by lack of data.

overflow_list.title: Aerial Booster-Cell Enabled Inter-Cell Interference Coordination for 5G NR Networks
overflow_list.authors: Md Sharif Hossen, Vijay K. Shah, Ismail Guvenc
overflow_list.abstract: Cellular-connected unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) operating in 5G New Radio (NR) macro networks experience severe and spatially non-uniform downlink interference. This is primarily caused by the interference from the sidelobes of downtilted base station (BS) antennas serving terrestrial users, which limits the ability of the network to provide uniform and high-quality coverage to aerial users. Supporting aerial users requires boosting the coverage of certain cells or sectors, which can further exacerbate inter-cell interference in dense macro deployments. This motivates the need for inter-cell interference coordination (ICIC) in multi-cell 5G NR networks serving both aerial and terrestrial users. In this work, we propose an ICIC framework that jointly optimizes antenna-domain coordination through BS uptilt angle optimization and time-domain interference coordination (TDIC) through NR-compliant scheduling. The framework is formulated as a multi-cell NR macro deployment problem that maximizes the minimum UAV signal-to-interference ratio (SIR) over a spatial grid of UAV locations while maintaining acceptable performance for ground user equipment (GUEs). The resulting optimization problem is non-convex and is solved using bio-inspired optimization techniques, including particle swarm optimization (PSO) and genetic algorithm (GA). Simulation results demonstrate that coordinated uptilt optimization with the booster-cell architecture significantly improves worst-case UAV SIR and downlink reliability in multi-cell 5G NR networks. booster-cell architecture significantly improves worst-case UAV SIR and downlink reliability in multi-cell 5G NR networks.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 蜂窝连接无人机在5G NR宏网络中面临严重的下行非均匀干扰，主要源于基站下倾天线旁瓣。本文提出联合优化天线上倾角与时域干扰协调(NR调度)的ICIC框架，以最大化无人机最小SIR并保持地面用户性能。采用PSO和GA求解非凸优化问题，仿真表明协调上倾优化与增强小区架构显著提升多小区5G NR网络中无人机最差SIR和下行可靠性。

strengths: Addresses a relevant and timely problem: UAV connectivity in 5G NR networks is an emerging use case with practical industry relevance
Proposes a joint optimization framework combining antenna-domain (uptilt) and time-domain coordination, which is methodologically sound
Formulates a max-min SIR problem that explicitly balances aerial and terrestrial user performance, showing awareness of trade-offs
Uses bio-inspired optimization (PSO, GA) which is appropriate for non-convex problems, though not novel in itself
limitations: Abstract only mentions 'simulation results' with no indication of real-world datasets, field measurements, or even realistic 3GPP channel models
No mention of baseline comparisons against state-of-the-art ICIC schemes (e.g., CoMP, eICIC, FeICIC) or 3GPP aerial UAV study items
Bio-inspired optimization (PSO/GA) is computationally expensive and may not be practical for real-time deployment in live networks
Lacks discussion of NR-specific features that could be leveraged (e.g., SSB-based measurements, beam management, RRC reconfiguration delays)
No quantitative results provided in abstract (e.g., 'X dB SIR improvement') making impact assessment difficult
Unclear how 'booster-cell' differs from standard cell range extension or antenna retuning
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Major concern: Abstract indicates pure simulation-based evaluation with no mention of real datasets, 3GPP-compliant channel models, or validation against field measurements. The term 'simulation results' without further qualification typically implies synthetic data. No comparison against established baselines mentioned.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: This is a solid but incremental contribution to aerial UAV communications in 5G. The problem is real and the joint optimization approach is reasonable, but the methodology relies on well-known heuristic techniques without clear validation against real-world data or established baselines. The lack of quantitative results and comparison details in the abstract limits confidence in the claims. Likely a mid-tier conference or journal paper (e.g., IEEE VTC, ICC workshops) rather than a top-tier venue.

overflow_list.title: Real-World LoRaWAN Performance and Propagation Modeling Using UAV, Helikite, and Vehicle-Based Measurements
overflow_list.authors: Sergio Vargas Villar, Simran Singh, Özgür Özdemir, Mihail L. Sichitiu, İsmail Güvenç
overflow_list.abstract: This paper presents a field-based evaluation of Long Range Wide Area Network (LoRaWAN) signal propagation conducted at two locations within the Aerial Experimentation and Research Platform for Advanced Wireless (AERPAW) testbed: Lake Wheeler Field and NC State University's Centennial Campus. Three distinct transmission platforms were deployed, a ground vehicle, a multirotor drone at 50 meters, and a helikite at a steady altitude of 150 meters and 300 meters approximately. These platforms enabled a comparative study on how altitude, mobility, and terrain influence wireless signal reception across a LoRaWAN gateway network. We analyze received signal strength (RSSI) and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) as functions of distance and spreading factor (SF). Three complementary metrics are visualized: SNR versus distance with demodulation thresholds, probability of successful reception, and SNR boxplots grouped by distance bins. These plots reveal link degradation patterns and demonstrate the role of adaptive SF selection in maintaining communication reliability. To characterize propagation behavior, we apply a log-distance path loss model to empirical data from the ground vehicle experiment, which encompass both rural and urban non-line-of-sight (NLOS) conditions. Model parameters are optimized through error minimization techniques. Our results show that the helikite platform, due to its stable high-altitude position, provided the most reliable and consistent link performance. Conversely, the drone and vehicle exhibited higher variability due to movement, obstructions, and terrain-induced multipath. These findings demonstrate the influence of platform dynamics and altitude on LoRaWAN reception performance, providing support for future aerial network planning efforts.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 基于AERPAW测试平台的LoRaWAN空对地信号传播特性研究。评估了地面车辆、多旋翼无人机（50m）和氦气飞艇（150-300m）三种发射平台对RSSI和SNR的影响，结果表明高海拔稳定平台（氦气飞艇）提供最可靠的链路性能，为空中网络规划提供实测依据。

strengths: Multi-platform comparative study (drone, helikite, ground vehicle) provides useful altitude/dynamics perspective for LoRaWAN — a practical and increasingly relevant scenario
Real field measurements at two distinct AERPAW testbed sites (rural lake + urban campus) covering both LOS and NLOS conditions — adds ecological validity
Analysis of both RSSI and SNR across varying spreading factors is appropriate for LoRaWAN characterization
Three complementary visualization approaches (SNR thresholds, reception probability, boxplots) cover the key angles of link quality assessment
Log-distance path loss model fitted to empirical data with error minimization — standard but sound propagation modeling approach
Results show helikite superiority due to stable altitude, which is a non-obvious and practically useful finding for network planners
limitations: Novelty is limited — multi-platform LoRaWAN propagation measurement studies have appeared in the literature (e.g., similar work exists for drone-to-ground links); the incremental contribution over existing work is unclear
No mention of number of measurement points, number of transmissions, repeatability, or statistical confidence intervals — cannot assess whether the dataset is sufficient to support the conclusions
Log-distance path loss model is the most basic propagation model; more advanced models (e.g., close-in reference, floating intercept, alpha-beta-gamma) are standard in the literature and absent here
Helikite at 'approximately' 150m and 300m suggests altitude may not have been precisely controlled — important for altitude-dependent propagation claims
No discussion of antenna characteristics (type, gain, polarization) — critical variables for reproducible propagation measurements
No mention of environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, foliage state) that affect 868/915 MHz propagation
The paper appears to be evaluation-only; no adaptive SF algorithm is proposed or tested, limiting the actionable value of the SF analysis
Abstract does not report key quantitative results (e.g., path loss exponents, typical SNR/RSSI values, range coverage), making it impossible to assess effect sizes from the abstract alone
Centroid vs. average SNR/RSSI treatment not discussed — important for understanding which metric was used in probability-of-reception calculations
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: The study uses real field measurements (not simulation), which is a significant strength over simulation-only papers. However, the abstract omits critical data quality indicators: number of measurement samples, transmission repetitions, environment descriptions, antenna specs, and statistical confidence bounds. The dataset scale is likely modest given it comes from three physical platforms at two sites — enough to demonstrate feasibility but likely insufficient for high-confidence propagation model parameter estimation. Baseline comparison with established propagation models or prior measurement campaigns is not mentioned, making it hard to gauge whether the fitted model is competitive.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: Overall score 58 reflects a solid real-world measurement contribution with practical insights for aerial LoRaWAN deployment, but limited by moderate novelty (incremental over existing propagation measurement work), absence of statistical rigor details, and a basic modeling approach. The practical impact score is boosted by the clear relevance to aerial network planning; the data score is real-world but flagged for lack of transparency about sample size and repeatability.

overflow_list.title: Modeling and Analysis of Air-to-Ground Cellular KPIs in a 5G Testbed using Android Smartphones
overflow_list.authors: Simran Singh, Anıl Gürses, Özgür Özdemir, Ram Asokan, Mihail L. Sichitiu, İsmail Güvenç, Rudra Dutta, Magreth Mushi
overflow_list.abstract: The integration of cellular communication with Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) extends the range of command and control and payload communications of autonomous UAV applications. Accurate modeling of this air-to-ground wireless environment aids UAV mission planning. Models built on and insights obtained from real-life experiments intricately capture the variations in air-to-ground link quality with UAV position, offering more fidelity for simulations and system design than those that rely on generic theoretical models designed for ground scenarios or ray-tracing simulations. In this work, we conduct aerial flights at the Aerial Experimentation and Research Platform for Advanced Wireless (AERPAW) Lake Wheeler testbed to study the variation in key performance indicators (KPIs) of a private 4G/5G cellular base station (BS) with the UAV's altitude, distance from the BS, elevation, and azimuth relative to the BS. Variations in 4G and 5G physical layer KPIs and application layer throughput are logged and analyzed, using two Android smartphones: a Keysight Nemo device, with enhanced KPI access, through a rooted operating system, and a standard smartphone running a custom application that utilizes open-source Android APIs. The observed signal strength measurements are compared to theoretical predictions from free space path loss models that incorporate the BS antenna radiation patterns. Mathematical model parameters for polynomial curve approximations are derived to fit the observed data. Light machine learning approaches, namely random forests, gradient boosting regressors and neural networks, are used to model KPI behaviour as a function of UAV position relative to the BS. The insights and models generated from real-life experiments in this study can serve as valuable tools in the design, simulation and deployment of cellular communication-based UAV systems.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 蜂窝通信与无人机的集成扩展了自主无人机应用的指挥控制和载荷通信范围。精确建模这种空对地无线环境有助于无人机任务规划。基于真实实验构建的模型和获得的洞察，能够精细捕捉空对地链路质量随无人机位置的变化，为仿真和系统设计提供比依赖地面场景通用理论模型或射线追踪仿真更高的保真度。本研究在AERPAW Lake Wheeler试验台进行空中飞行实验，研究私有4G/5G蜂窝基站的关键性能指标(KPI)随无人机高度、与基站距离、仰角和方位角的变化。使用两部安卓智能手机记录并分析4G和5G物理层KPI及应用层吞吐量的变化：一部是Keysight Nemo设备，通过root系统增强KPI访问能力；另一部是运行利用开源安卓API的定制应用的标准智能手机。将观测到的信号强度测量值与结合基站天线辐射图型的自由空间路径损耗模型理论预测进行对比。推导多项式曲线逼近的数学模型参数以拟合观测数据。采用轻量级机器学习方法（随机森林、梯度提升回归器和神经网络）建立KPI随无人机相对基站位置变化的模型。本研究真实实验产生的洞察和模型可作为基于蜂窝通信的无人机系统设计、仿真和部署的宝贵工具。

strengths: Real-world aerial flight experiments at a dedicated testbed (AERPAW), not purely simulation-based — significant strength for data quality
Comparison of two Android devices (rooted Keysight Nemo vs. standard smartphone with open-source APIs) adds practical insight into measurement accessibility
Multi-dimensional analysis (altitude, distance, elevation, azimuth) provides a comprehensive spatial characterization of A2G channels
Comparison with theoretical free-space path loss models incorporating BS antenna radiation patterns anchors empirical findings against established theory
Multiple modeling approaches (polynomial curve fitting + ML: RF, GBR, NN) provide flexibility for different use cases
limitations: Novelty is limited — air-to-ground channel measurement campaigns with UAVs have been extensively studied; the incremental contribution is primarily the testbed and device comparison rather than new methodology or fundamental insight
Uses a private 4G/5G BS in a single testbed environment (Lake Wheeler) — generalizability to urban, suburban, or other deployments is uncertain
ML models (RF, GBR, NN) described as 'light' — unclear if they offer meaningful improvement over simpler parametric models; risk of overfitting to a limited flight dataset
Abstract does not clearly articulate what new insights were discovered beyond 'variations exist with position' — the key findings are not highlighted
No mention of dataset size (number of flights, data points), which is critical for assessing the robustness of the derived models
Polynomial curve approximations are a basic fitting technique; without cross-validation or out-of-sample validation details, their utility is questionable
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: The paper uses real-world flight data from a dedicated testbed (AERPAW), which is a genuine strength. Two different measurement devices (rooted and non-rooted Android) add robustness. However, the data comes from a single BS in a single environment, and the abstract does not disclose the scale of the dataset (number of flights, measurement duration, data points). The comparison against free-space path loss with antenna patterns is appropriate but standard. Overall: real data elevates this above purely simulation papers, but limited scope and unknown dataset size prevent a higher score.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: The paper's main value lies in real-world A2G measurement data from a unique testbed, but novelty is modest — this is fundamentally a measurement campaign paper with standard ML fitting on top. The rigor appears adequate (multi-device, multi-variable, comparison to theory) but unexceptional. Practical impact is moderate: useful for the AERPAW community and cellular-UAV system designers, but single-site results limit generalizability. The abstract is well-structured but fails to highlight concrete findings, slightly hurting presentation.

overflow_list.title: A Family of Open Time-Series Foundation Models for the Radio Access Network
overflow_list.authors: Ioannis Panitsas, Leandros Tassiulas
overflow_list.abstract: The Radio Access Network (RAN) is evolving into a programmable and disaggregated infrastructure that increasingly relies on AI-native algorithms for optimization and closed-loop control. However, current RAN intelligence is still largely built from task-specific models tailored to individual functions, resulting in model fragmentation, limited knowledge sharing across tasks, poor generalization, and increased system complexity. To address these limitations, we introduce TimeRAN, a unified multi-task learning framework for time-series modeling in the RAN. TimeRAN leverages a lightweight time-series foundation model with few task-specific heads to learn transferable representations that can be efficiently adapted across diverse tasks with limited supervision. To enable large-scale pretraining, we further curate and open-source TimeRAN DataPile, the largest time-series corpus for RAN analytics to date, comprising over 355K time series and 0.56B measurements across diverse telemetry sources, protocol layers, and deployment scenarios. We evaluate TimeRAN across a comprehensive set of RAN analytics tasks, including anomaly detection, classification, forecasting, and imputation, and show that it achieves state-of-the-art performance with minimal or no task-specific fine-tuning. Finally, we integrate TimeRAN into a proof-of-concept 5G testbed and demonstrate that it operates efficiently with limited resource requirements in real-world scenarios.
overflow_list.summary_cn: 提出TimeRAN统一多任务学习框架，利用轻量化时序基础模型学习可迁移表示，支持异常检测、分类、预测等多任务，并开源迄今最大RAN时序数据集TimeRAN DataPile（355K时序、5.6亿测量点）。

strengths: First time-series foundation model specifically designed for RAN domain
Multi-task learning approach addresses model fragmentation in current RAN AI systems
TimeRAN DataPile is the largest open-source RAN time-series corpus (355K series, 0.56B measurements)
Comprehensive evaluation across four diverse tasks (anomaly detection, classification, forecasting, imputation)
Real-world validation via 5G testbed integration demonstrating practical feasibility
Open-source contribution enables reproducibility and community adoption
limitations: Abstract does not specify baseline comparison details or performance margins
Limited information on model architecture complexity and inference latency
Testbed proof-of-concept may not reflect full-scale deployment challenges
Domain-specific foundation model may have limited transferability beyond RAN
quality_assessment.data_quality_note: Strong data quality score. The TimeRAN DataPile represents a substantial contribution with 355K real-world time series from diverse telemetry sources, protocol layers, and deployment scenarios. Open-sourcing the dataset significantly enhances reproducibility and enables future research. This addresses a critical gap in RAN analytics where real-world datasets are typically proprietary and scarce.
quality_assessment.prediction_reason: This paper makes a compelling contribution to the emerging field of AI-native RAN. The foundation model paradigm applied to RAN time-series is timely and addresses genuine industry pain points (model fragmentation, limited knowledge sharing). The creation and open-sourcing of a large-scale RAN dataset is a significant contribution that will likely catalyze further research. The combination of strong novelty, real-world data, and practical testbed validation suggests high acceptance probability. Score of 82 reflects solid work with clear industry relevance, though technical depth of baselines and scalability claims would need verification in the full paper.
